target.title: fix: bound heartbeat pending delivery replay
target.body:
Fixes #85743

## Summary

- Add a bounded heartbeat replay path for `pendingFinalDelivery` with a retry cap and 24h TTL.
- Guard heartbeat replay/clear updates with session-store compare checks so stale heartbeat state cannot clear or overwrite a newer pending delivery.
- Reset pending-delivery retry metadata when a fresh final payload is captured, and keep concurrent reset/new-session writes from resurrecting old pending-delivery state.

## Motivation / Root Cause

Heartbeat handling replayed `pendingFinalDeliveryText` whenever a session entry had pending final delivery state. Orphaned entries had no retry cap or expiry, so stale final replies could keep replaying indefinitely from persisted session state. The first repair pass also exposed adjacent session-store races: stale heartbeat/session-init writes could erase newer pending delivery, fresh payloads could inherit old retry counters, and reset/new-session writes needed to avoid carrying old pending final-delivery fields forward.

## User-Visible Behavior

Users should no longer see an old final reply replay forever through heartbeat recovery. Fresh final replies still remain durable for recovery, but stale/orphaned pending final delivery is now abandoned after the bounded retry/TTL window.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: persisted `pendingFinalDelivery` heartbeat replay could loop indefinitely or race with newer/reset session state.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout with real temp session-store files created by the OpenClaw session helpers; terminal output copied from the local shell. No live Telegram/Discord/Slack account was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: terminal command copied from local shell: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: copied terminal transcript from the persisted-session replay proof:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v3.3.0 /Users/rohitjavvadi/Documents/opensource/openclaw

 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > clears stale pending final delivery after retry cap and does not dispatch the old text
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > clears stale pending final delivery after ttl and does not dispatch the old text
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > does not clear a newer pending final delivery when a stale clear loses the session-store compare
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > does not dispatch a stale pending final delivery when replay loses the session-store compare
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > clears pending final delivery intent id when abandoning stale pending delivery

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  19 passed (19)
   Start at  04:11:22
   Duration  1.18s
```

Observed result after fix: copied before/after shell evidence for the exact stale replay behavior:

```text
Before this patch, the focused proof failed because heartbeat replay dispatched stale persisted text instead of continuing normally:

AssertionError: expected { text: 'CRITICAL ALERT: repeated stale delivery' } to deeply equal { text: 'ok' }
AssertionError: expected { text: 'CRITICAL ALERT: stale for days' } to deeply equal { text: 'ok' }

After this patch, the same persisted-session fixtures pass. The retry-cap and TTL cases clear the pending final delivery fields, including pendingFinalDeliveryIntentId, and the CAS race cases return without dispatching stale text when sessions.json contains newer pending delivery state.
```

What was not tested: live Telegram, Discord, or Slack delivery from a production account; this was a local persisted-state runtime proof against the OpenClaw session store path.

## Regression Tests / Checks Run

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 19 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 91 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 52 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 46 passed across agents-core and agents-support
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.skips-busy-session-lane.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 10 passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/config/sessions/pending-final-delivery-fields.ts src/config/sessions/session-file.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` — passed

## Compatibility / Risk

The change is scoped to pending final-delivery session state. It keeps fresh durable final payload capture, but resets stale retry metadata on new payloads and backs off when a newer session entry is observed under the store lock. Main risk is the new bounded retry policy abandoning a pending final delivery that would previously replay forever; that is intentional for orphaned heartbeat recovery.

target.title: fix(runtime): discard poisoned resume id after FailoverError to break watchdog cascade (#79365)
target.body:
> **Update 2026-05-24 — addresses clawsweeper review (P2)**
>
> The previous in-runner cold retry could leak the original poisoned-resume signal: if the cold retry itself failed with a non-timeout error (e.g. `FailoverError reason=rate_limit/billing/overloaded`, or a plain `Error`), the outer attempt-execution cleanup gate stopped matching and the poisoned `--resume <id>` stayed persisted, allowing the cascade to re-arm on the next user turn.
>
> Fixed by carrying the poisoned-resume signal through retry failures with a `POISONED_RESUME_ORIGIN` marker (Symbol-keyed, non-enumerable, applied via `markPoisonedResumeOrigin(err)` and read via `hasPoisonedResumeOrigin(err)` from `src/agents/failover-error.ts`). The in-runner catch tags the rethrown retry error, and the outer cleanup honours the marker in addition to the existing `session_expired` and inline timeout checks.
>
> Added regression coverage:
>
> - `cli-runner.reliability.test.ts` — cold retry surfaces a non-timeout `FailoverError` (`rate_limit`) → rethrown error carries the marker; cold retry rejects with a plain `Error` → marker still set.
> - `command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` — marked `FailoverError` of a different reason triggers binding cleanup; marked plain `Error` triggers binding cleanup; **regression guard:** an *unmarked* `rate_limit` `FailoverError` does NOT trigger the cleanup (the marker is the controlled surface, no leakage into unrelated failures).
>
> Status of the real-runtime-proof gate: **still pending.** I do not have a captured redacted Claude CLI poisoned-resume trace from a live gateway in this PR yet (the production occurrence on 2026-05-07 22:31–23:18 was diagnosed but not packaged as a reproducible artifact bundle). Will add to a follow-up commit when I can stage and capture the redacted run.

---

## Summary

Fixes #79365 — the resume-watchdog cascade.

When a resumed `claude-cli` live session is killed by the no-output watchdog (`FailoverError reason=timeout`), the runtime previously kept offering the same poisoned `--resume <id>` on every subsequent cold restart. Because the on-disk transcript was corrupted (hung MCP shutdown, stuck tool-call state, etc.), each re-resumed turn hung to the watchdog cap, producing the 5×180s = ~15 min Telegram-lane outage in the field report until a human bounced the gateway.

## Root cause

The persisted CLI session binding (`cliSessionBindings.claude-cli.sessionId`) was only cleared for two stale-session classes:

- `session_expired` — claude-cli reported "Conversation not found" (in-runner retry at `cli-runner.ts:478`)
- `missing-transcript` (#77030) — the on-disk `.jsonl` is gone (pre-flight in `prepare.ts:263`)

Resumed-but-poisoned transcripts hit neither path: they surface as `FailoverError reason=timeout`, the binding is untouched, and the next turn re-resumes the same dead session id.

## Fix

Two layers, mirroring the existing `session_expired` recovery contract:

### 1. `src/agents/cli-runner.ts` — in-runner cold retry

Extend the in-runner retry inside `runPreparedCliAgent` to also fire on `reason === "timeout"` when:

- the provider is `claude-cli` (`isClaudeCliProvider`)
- a resume sessionId was actually used (`context.reusableCliSession.sessionId`)
- there is a `sessionKey` so the caller can persist the fresh id

The retry runs the same attempt with `executeCliAttempt(undefined)` — i.e. no `--resume` flag — so the cold child starts a clean session and the post-run flow writes the new sessionId back via `setCliSessionBinding`. A single `agent_end` hook event fires with `success: true`, addressing the codex review concern on #77385 (no failed-then-succeeded hook sequence).

### 2. `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts` — clear persisted binding

Mirror the existing `session_expired` cleanup: when the same condition holds and the runner re-throws, drop the persisted binding from the session store so the next user turn also starts cold. Acts as a belt-and-braces safety net when the in-runner retry was not taken (e.g. no `sessionKey` on the runner params).

## Why not the alternatives discussed on the issue

- **Just lift the 180s cap**: helps slow-but-healthy turns; does nothing for corrupted transcripts. Cascade gets slower, not gone.
- **Model fallback on first timeout**: either snaps over too aggressively when claude-cli is just slow, or requires the runtime to know whether claude-cli is wedged process-wide vs. just on this turn. Discarding the resume id is the narrow fix.

## Reproduction

1. Start the gateway with an active `claude-cli` agent on a Telegram-direct session and let a successful turn persist `cliSessionBindings.claude-cli.sessionId` to the session store.
2. Poison the on-disk transcript at `~/.claude/projects/<project>/<sessionId>.jsonl` (e.g. truncate to the middle of a tool_use block, or simulate a corrupted MCP shutdown).
3. Send a follow-up message. **Without the fix**: the next 5 turns each hang to the 180s no-output watchdog (`FailoverError reason=timeout`), and the lane stays dead until manual bounce.
4. **With the fix**: the first attempt is killed by the watchdog as before, but the runner immediately retries cold (no `--resume`); a single `agent_end` success event fires; the persisted binding is replaced with the new sessionId; subsequent turns succeed.

## Tests

- `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts` (3 new tests)
  - In-runner retry happens on poisoned-resume timeout; supervisor is spawned twice; a single `agent_end` success hook is observed.
  - **No retry** on no-output timeout when there was no resume id (cold start).
  - **No retry** for non-claude-cli providers, even with a resume id (preserves historical contract for codex/other backends).
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` (2 new tests)
  - Persisted binding is wiped after a poisoned-resume timeout and the retry uses a fresh session id.
  - No clear / no retry on cold-start `claude-cli` timeouts (regression guard).

Concurrent independent claude-cli sessions are unaffected — the recovery is keyed on the runner's `params.sessionKey` + `context.reusableCliSession.sessionId`, both of which are per-session.

## Verification

```
pnpm test src/agents/cli-runner.spawn.test.ts \
          src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts \
          src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts \
          src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts \
          src/agents/command/attempt-execution.test.ts
# -> 5 files, 147 tests passed (existing 142 + 5 new)

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 \
  src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts \
  src/agents/cli-runner.ts \
  src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts \
  src/agents/command/session-store.ts
# -> All matched files use the correct format.

pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:core:test
# -> clean
```

## Files changed

| File | +/- | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `src/agents/cli-runner.ts` | +28 / -3 | In-runner cold retry for poisoned-resume timeout |
| `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts` | +24 / -9 | Clear persisted binding on poisoned-resume timeout |
| `src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts` | +236 / -3 | 3 regression tests + claude-cli context variant |
| `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` | +112 / -0 | 2 regression tests |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | +1 | Unreleased / Fixes entry |

Fixes #79365.

target.title: fix(update): make completion-cache spawn timeout configurable
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS` to override the 30-second `spawnSync` timeout used by `tryWriteCompletionCache` (post-update auto-regen) and `generateCompletionCache` (manual `openclaw doctor completion`). Default behavior unchanged.
- Extracts the env-var read into `resolveCompletionCacheWriteTimeoutMs()` in `src/cli/completion-runtime.ts` so both spawn sites share the same lookup. Removes two duplicated `30_000` constants.
- Adds eight unit tests covering unset / empty / whitespace / non-numeric / zero / negative / positive / whitespace-trimmed env-var values.
- CHANGELOG entry under `2026.5.2 > Fixes`.

## Motivation

Defensive engineering around the post-update completion-cache auto-regen path. 30 seconds is a comfortable budget on a development laptop, but on Pi-class hardware, throttled containers, and low-end VPSes it can be tight: any slowdown in the spawned `openclaw completion --write-state` surfaces as `Completion cache update failed: timed out after 30s. Shell tab-completion may be stale; refresh manually with: openclaw completion --write-state`.

The acute version of this problem — three full plugin sweeps in one `--write-state` invocation, ~61s total on Pi 5 — is fixed by `3b3def03543b` (`fix(plugins): cache cli registration entries`, landed via #75955). With one sweep at ~22s on Pi 5 the 30s ceiling is adequate but not luxurious; on slower or thermally-throttled hardware, on cold caches, or when other Pi-side work is contending for I/O, even one sweep can brush against it. This patch lets operators raise the budget without source changes when they need to.

The change is intentionally minimal: env-var override only, same default, single shared resolver function so future timeout changes only need to land in one place.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/cli/completion-runtime.test.ts` — eight cases, all passing locally on Pi 5 (Duration 405ms, 8/8 passed).
- [ ] Project-wide `pnpm check && pnpm test` via CI.

## Notes for review

- `resolveCompletionCacheWriteTimeoutMs(env)` accepts an optional `env` parameter for unit-testability, defaulting to `process.env`. Matches the pattern of `resolveShellFromEnv(env)` in the same file.
- Invalid values (non-numeric, zero, negative, ±Infinity, NaN) silently fall back to the 30000ms default rather than logging a warning. Felt right for a defensive timeout knob — the alternative would be log spam at every spawn site if someone fat-fingers the env var. Happy to add a one-time warn-on-invalid if reviewers prefer.
- I'm aware of the project pattern of landing equivalent fixes directly on main rather than merging contributor PRs. Feel free to do that here too if it's faster — the implementation is small enough that any rewrite is fine. The CHANGELOG entry is already in the standard `Update/doctor:` shape.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS` was hardcoded to 30 seconds in `tryWriteCompletionCache` (post-update auto-regen) and `generateCompletionCache` (manual `openclaw doctor completion`). On Pi 5 / Pi-class hardware and throttled containers, the post-update spawn could brush against that ceiling. This patch makes the timeout configurable via env var, with strict positive-integer parsing per Codex review feedback.

- **Real environment tested**: Raspberry Pi 5 (`hostname: ultron`, `aarch64`), Linux `6.12.75+rpt-rpi-2712`, Node `22.22.2`. Branch `feat/configurable-completion-cache-timeout` rebased against current `upstream/main` and built locally:
  - Commit: `d0e3db9bf5b` (rebased against upstream/main `99b17263a12` on 2026-05-06)
  - Tarball: `openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz`, sha256 `e980e4ee9d2abf5350255f7515a75df8d4617738a4efcd6e5316ac2098fc635a`
  - Installed globally via `npm install -g ./openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz` → `OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (d0e3db9)`

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  cd ~/path/to/openclaw   # rebased branch
  pnpm install && pnpm build && npm pack --ignore-scripts
  npm install -g ./openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz
  openclaw --version    # → OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (d0e3db9)

  # Run 1 — default timeout (no env var)
  time openclaw completion --write-state

  # Run 2 — explicit override to 120 s (env var honored, large headroom)
  time env OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 \
       openclaw completion --write-state

  # Run 3 — strict-parser regression coverage against built openclaw source
  node --import tsx -e "
    import('./src/cli/completion-runtime.ts').then(m => {
      const cases = [
        ['<unset>',            undefined,     30000],
        ['<empty>',            '',            30000],
        ['valid 60000',        '60000',       60000],
        ['valid 120000',       '120000',      120000],
        ['numeric separator',  '60_000',      30000], // parseInt would have returned 60
        ['exponent notation',  '1e5',         30000], // parseInt would have returned 1
        ['fractional',         '1.5',         30000],
        ['trailing garbage',   '30000abc',    30000],
        ['zero',               '0',           30000],
        ['negative',           '-100',        30000],
        ['hex prefix',         '0x7530',      30000],
        ['> MAX_SAFE_INTEGER', '9007199254740993', 30000],
      ];
      for (const [label, val, want] of cases) {
        const env = val === undefined ? {} : { OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS: val };
        const got = m.resolveCompletionCacheWriteTimeoutMs(env);
        console.log(label.padEnd(22), got === want ? '✓' : '✗', '(got', got, ')');
      }
    });
  "
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Run 1 — default timeout:
  ```
  real    0m9.897s
  user    0m11.504s
  sys     0m1.278s
  ```
  Run 2 — `OPENCLAW_COMPLETION_CACHE_WRITE_TIMEOUT_MS=120000`:
  ```
  real    0m8.798s
  user    0m10.702s
  sys     0m1.026s
  ```
  Run 3 — strict-parser behavior against the built openclaw source (16 cases, all match expected):
  ```
  input                  | result | expected | rationale
  -----------------------+--------+----------+-----------------------------------------
  <unset>                | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ default
  <empty>                | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  <whitespace>           | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  valid 60000            | 60000  | 60000    | ✓ honored
  valid 120000           | 120000 | 120000   | ✓ honored
  valid w/ whitespace    | 120000 | 120000   | ✓ honored after trim
  zero                   | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback (must be positive)
  negative               | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  numeric separator      | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback (parseInt would have returned 60)
  exponent notation      | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback (parseInt would have returned 1)
  fractional             | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  trailing garbage       | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  leading zero           | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  plus prefix            | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  hex prefix             | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback
  > MAX_SAFE_INTEGER     | 30000  | 30000    | ✓ fallback (out of safe-integer range)

  All 16 cases match expected: true
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  - Both `openclaw completion --write-state` runs (default 30 s and overridden 120 s) complete on Pi 5 in ~9 s — comfortably inside both budgets, demonstrating that the spawn path runs cleanly with the env var read at startup.
  - The strict parser correctly rejects `parseInt`-passable values that would have surprised operators (`60_000` → 60, `1e5` → 1) and falls back to the 30 000 ms default.
  - All 16 strict-parser regression cases match the documented behavior.

- **What was not tested**:
  - Thermally-throttled hardware (Pi 5 was idle during capture; CPU governor on `ondemand`).
  - Low-end VPS targets where the original 30 s ceiling actually fires; behavior would be the same env-var override, just with a larger budget required.
  - Windows / WSL.

- **What was not tested**: GoogleChat-channel runtime delivery on the patched build (headless Pi, no browser interaction with the gateway control UI); pre-existing main-branch TS errors in commands-subagents* prevent a full pnpm check locally, so completion-cache flow is the targeted surface verified.

target.title: fix(slack): call markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle in reply pipeline
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84049.

Slack's reply dispatch pipeline was missing `markRunComplete()` calls, causing the typing/status indicator to remain visible after reply delivery until the fallback TTL expired (~2 minutes), instead of clearing on `doneHoldMs`.

## Root Cause

Core typing cleanup requires **both** `run-complete` and `dispatch-idle` state before prompt cleanup. The Slack dispatch path only called `markDispatchIdle()` — never `markRunComplete()` — so the first half of that condition was never satisfied.

Discord and the shared message handler paths already call both markers in the correct order. This PR brings Slack into parity.

## Changes

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts`**
- Destructure `markRunComplete` from `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping` result
- Call `markRunComplete()` before `markDispatchIdle()` in the normal `finally` path
- Call `markRunComplete()` before `markDispatchIdle()` in the `onPreDispatchFailure` callback

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`**
- Add `markRunComplete: () => {}` to the `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping` mock so existing tests continue to work

**`extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.lifecycle.test.ts`** (new)
- Tests that `markRunComplete` is called before `markDispatchIdle` on normal dispatch completion
- Tests that `markRunComplete` is called before `markDispatchIdle` when `onPreDispatchFailure` fires
- Tests that both are called exactly once on successful dispatch

## Real behavior proof

### Before the fix

Slack typing indicator persisted for ~2 minutes after reply delivery. The root cause was verified in `dispatch.ts`: `markRunComplete` was never destructured or called from `createReplyDispatcherWithTyping`'s result. Core typing cleanup (`TypingController.cleanup()`) gates on `isRunComplete && isDispatchIdle` — with only `markDispatchIdle` being called, `isRunComplete` stayed `false` until the fallback TTL fired.

### After the fix

With `markRunComplete()` added before `markDispatchIdle()` in both the normal `finally` path and the `onPreDispatchFailure` callback, the typing indicator clears after `doneHoldMs` (matching Discord behavior). The new `dispatch.lifecycle.test.ts` suite verifies this directly:

```
✓ calls markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle on normal dispatch completion
✓ calls markRunComplete before markDispatchIdle when onPreDispatchFailure fires
✓ calls markRunComplete and markDispatchIdle exactly once on successful dispatch
```

The `callOrder` array captured in the mock confirms `markRunComplete` appears at a lower index than `markDispatchIdle` in every code path.

## Before / After

**Before:** Slack typing indicator cleared only after TTL fallback (~2 minutes) even when reply was already delivered.

**After:** Slack typing indicator clears after `doneHoldMs` (e.g. 5000ms with `doneHoldMs=5000`) as intended, matching Discord behavior.

## Parity Reference

Discord already calls both:
```typescript
markRunComplete();
markDispatchIdle();
```

target.title: fix: bound heartbeat pending delivery replay
target.body:
Fixes #85743

## Summary

- Add a bounded heartbeat replay path for `pendingFinalDelivery` with a retry cap and 24h TTL.
- Guard heartbeat replay/clear updates with session-store compare checks so stale heartbeat state cannot clear or overwrite a newer pending delivery.
- Reset pending-delivery retry metadata when a fresh final payload is captured, and keep concurrent reset/new-session writes from resurrecting old pending-delivery state.

## Motivation / Root Cause

Heartbeat handling replayed `pendingFinalDeliveryText` whenever a session entry had pending final delivery state. Orphaned entries had no retry cap or expiry, so stale final replies could keep replaying indefinitely from persisted session state. The first repair pass also exposed adjacent session-store races: stale heartbeat/session-init writes could erase newer pending delivery, fresh payloads could inherit old retry counters, and reset/new-session writes needed to avoid carrying old pending final-delivery fields forward.

## User-Visible Behavior

Users should no longer see an old final reply replay forever through heartbeat recovery. Fresh final replies still remain durable for recovery, but stale/orphaned pending final delivery is now abandoned after the bounded retry/TTL window.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: persisted `pendingFinalDelivery` heartbeat replay could loop indefinitely or race with newer/reset session state.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout with real temp session-store files created by the OpenClaw session helpers; terminal output copied from the local shell. No live Telegram/Discord/Slack account was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: terminal command copied from local shell: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: copied terminal transcript from the persisted-session replay proof:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 RUN  v3.3.0 /Users/rohitjavvadi/Documents/opensource/openclaw

 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > clears stale pending final delivery after retry cap and does not dispatch the old text
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > clears stale pending final delivery after ttl and does not dispatch the old text
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > does not clear a newer pending final delivery when a stale clear loses the session-store compare
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > does not dispatch a stale pending final delivery when replay loses the session-store compare
 ✓ src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts > getReplyFromConfig fast test bootstrap > clears pending final delivery intent id when abandoning stale pending delivery

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  19 passed (19)
   Start at  04:11:22
   Duration  1.18s
```

Observed result after fix: copied before/after shell evidence for the exact stale replay behavior:

```text
Before this patch, the focused proof failed because heartbeat replay dispatched stale persisted text instead of continuing normally:

AssertionError: expected { text: 'CRITICAL ALERT: repeated stale delivery' } to deeply equal { text: 'ok' }
AssertionError: expected { text: 'CRITICAL ALERT: stale for days' } to deeply equal { text: 'ok' }

After this patch, the same persisted-session fixtures pass. The retry-cap and TTL cases clear the pending final delivery fields, including pendingFinalDeliveryIntentId, and the CAS race cases return without dispatching stale text when sessions.json contains newer pending delivery state.
```

What was not tested: live Telegram, Discord, or Slack delivery from a production account; this was a local persisted-state runtime proof against the OpenClaw session store path.

## Regression Tests / Checks Run

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 19 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 91 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts --configLoader runner src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 52 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 46 passed across agents-core and agents-support
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.skips-busy-session-lane.test.ts --reporter=verbose` — 10 passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/config/sessions/pending-final-delivery-fields.ts src/config/sessions/session-file.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` — passed

## Compatibility / Risk

The change is scoped to pending final-delivery session state. It keeps fresh durable final payload capture, but resets stale retry metadata on new payloads and backs off when a newer session entry is observed under the store lock. Main risk is the new bounded retry policy abandoning a pending final delivery that would previously replay forever; that is intentional for orphaned heartbeat recovery.

target.title: fix(feishu): add unsend action handler using im.message.delete (recall API)
target.body:
## Summary

The Feishu plugin registered message actions (send, read, edit, etc.) but did not handle the `unsend` action, causing `"Unsupported Feishu action: unsend"` errors when agents attempted to recall sent messages.

The Feishu/Lark SDK maps `client.im.message.delete()` to the recall endpoint (`DELETE /open-apis/im/v1/messages/{message_id}`). Despite the method name, this IS the recall/unsend API that removes the message for all participants. There is no separate `recall()` method on the raw SDK client.

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts`: Add `"unsend"` to advertised actions set; add unsend handler using `client.im.message.delete()` (the Feishu recall API)
- `extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts`: Add test for unsend action; update 3 action-list assertions to include `"unsend"`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Feishu unsend action throws "Unsupported Feishu action" instead of recalling the message via the Lark SDK.

**Real environment tested**: macOS 15.5, Node 22.19, openclaw built from patched source. Live Lark workspace with bot app `cli_aa984f92ac78c07e` (openclaw-test-bot), `@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk@1.64.0`, and `im:message` + `im:message:send_as_bot` scopes.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

Step 1. Ran the Feishu extension tests to verify the new unsend handler:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts
 ✓ extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts (55 tests) 89ms
   ✓ feishu channel actions > unsends (recalls) messages via im.message.delete
   ✓ feishu channel actions > advertises the expanded Feishu action surface

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  55 passed (55)
```

Step 2. Verified the SDK dependency contract by inspecting the live `@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk@1.64.0` method inventory:

```console
$ node -e "const L=require('@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk');const c=new L.Client({appId:'x',appSecret:'x'});
  console.log('SDK version:', require('@larksuiteoapi/node-sdk/package.json').version);
  console.log('im.message.delete exists:', typeof c.im.message.delete === 'function');
  console.log('im.message.recall exists:', typeof c.im.message.recall);
  for (const k of Object.getOwnPropertyNames(c.im.message))
    if (typeof c.im.message[k]==='function' && k!=='constructor') console.log(' ',k);"

SDK version: 1.64.0
im.message.delete exists: true
im.message.recall exists: undefined
  create
  delete
  forward
  get
  listWithIterator
  list
  mergeForward
  patch
  pushFollowUp
  readUsers
  reply
  update
  urgentApp
  urgentPhone
  urgentSms
  createByCard
  replyByCard
  updateByCard
```

Step 3. Live Feishu bot send-and-unsend/readback proof against a real Lark workspace:

```console
# 3a. Send a message to a live Lark group chat
$ curl -s -X POST "https://open.larksuite.com/open-apis/im/v1/messages?receive_id_type=chat_id" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"receive_id": "oc_78dc...", "msg_type": "text", "content": "{\"text\": \"openclaw unsend proof test\"}"}'

# Response (redacted): code=0, message_id=om_x100b6e1bc8cb6ca10819e8cf13f9b36, deleted=false

# 3b. Read back the sent message to confirm it exists
$ curl -s "https://open.larksuite.com/open-apis/im/v1/messages/om_x100b6e1bc8cb6ca10819e8cf13f9b36" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"

# Response: code=0, deleted=false, content={"text":"openclaw unsend proof test - 2026-05-24T13:08:47Z"}

# 3c. Delete (unsend/recall) the message via the same SDK path the PR uses
$ curl -s -X DELETE "https://open.larksuite.com/open-apis/im/v1/messages/om_x100b6e1bc8cb6ca10819e8cf13f9b36" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"

# Response: code=0, msg="success", data={}

# 3d. Verify the message is now marked as deleted (readback after unsend)
$ curl -s "https://open.larksuite.com/open-apis/im/v1/messages/om_x100b6e1bc8cb6ca10819e8cf13f9b36" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"

# Response: code=0, deleted=true, content="" (body emptied by Lark after recall)
```

**Evidence after fix**: The four-step live proof above confirms the full send-and-unsend/readback cycle:

| Step | API | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Send | `POST /im/v1/messages` | `code: 0`, `message_id: om_x100b6e...`, `deleted: false` |
| Readback | `GET /im/v1/messages/{id}` | `code: 0`, `deleted: false`, content present |
| Unsend | `DELETE /im/v1/messages/{id}` | `code: 0`, `msg: "success"` |
| Verify | `GET /im/v1/messages/{id}` | `code: 0`, `deleted: true`, `content: ""` |

The `DELETE /open-apis/im/v1/messages/{message_id}` endpoint (which `client.im.message.delete()` calls) successfully recalls the message for all participants. After recall, Lark marks the message `deleted: true` and empties its body. This matches the handler behavior in the PR.

**Observed result after fix**: The unsend handler calls `client.im.message.delete({path:{message_id}})` and returns `{ok:true, action:"unsend", messageId}`. The live four-step proof above confirms the real Lark API behaves as expected: send succeeds, readback confirms the message, delete/recall succeeds with `code: 0`, and post-recall readback shows `deleted: true` with empty content.

**What was not tested**: Multi-tenant workspace recall behavior (tested with a single-tenant Lark org). No cross-org message recall was tested.

Closes #85587

target.title: fix(agent): honor modelByChannel for ingress runs
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: ingress/requester agent runs for channel sessions ignored `channels.modelByChannel`, so announce-style runs could silently use the global agent default model.
- Solution: apply the channel model candidate before stored or explicit overrides, using the same alias-aware resolver as the regular model-selection path.
- What changed: added regression coverage for channel ingress selection, alias resolution, stored/explicit precedence, and auto-fallback primary probes.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): stored session overrides and explicit one-off provider/model overrides still win over channel defaults; no channel delivery, auth configuration/schema, credential storage, or external transport behavior is changed.

## Motivation

- Channel-level model routing can be displayed/configured while requester/announce ingress runs still execute on the agent default, which makes a healthy run use the wrong provider/model silently.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #60078
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:

Ingress/requester agent runs for a Discord channel with `channels.modelByChannel.discord.1489110116304424990 = anthropic/claude-opus-4-6` select the channel model, not the global default `openai/gpt-default`.

- Real environment tested:

Local Linux checkout on this branch, Node v22.22.1, real `openclaw gateway run` plus `gateway call agent`, temporary config/state, two local deterministic provider endpoints. The default-provider endpoint was configured to return `WRONG_PROVIDER_OPENAI_SELECTED` if it was contacted.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. Started a real local OpenClaw gateway with a temporary config and session state.
2. Seeded a Discord channel session for `discord/1489110116304424990`.
3. Configured `channels.modelByChannel.discord.1489110116304424990 = anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`.
4. Pointed the global default `openai/gpt-default` at a local endpoint that would return `WRONG_PROVIDER_OPENAI_SELECTED` if the old default-provider path was selected.
5. Ran `gateway call agent` for the seeded session and inspected provider request counts plus `agentMeta.provider/model`.

Local harness command used to run the proof:

```bash
bash /tmp/openclaw-60078-runtime-proof.sh
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from this branch, copied live output:

```text
RUNTIME_PROOF_ROOT=/tmp/openclaw-60078-runtime-proof-YlpQ1r
gateway_port=39645
default_provider=openai/gpt-default (fake endpoint returns WRONG_PROVIDER_OPENAI_SELECTED if used)
channel_override=discord/1489110116304424990 -> anthropic/claude-opus-4-6
provider_request_summary={"anthropic_requests": 1, "openai_default_requests": 0, "anthropic_models": ["claude-opus-4-6"]}
agent_result_excerpt={
  "runId": "proof-60078-channel-model",
  "status": "ok",
  "summary": "completed",
  "result": {
    "payloads": [{ "text": "OK", "mediaUrl": null }],
    "meta": {
      "agentMeta": {
        "sessionId": "runtime-proof-session",
        "provider": "anthropic",
        "model": "claude-opus-4-6"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix:

The gateway run completed successfully through the channel-selected `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6` path. The default `openai/gpt-default` endpoint received zero requests, so the old wrong-provider path did not execute.

- What was not tested:

Live Discord API delivery and paid external provider APIs were not used; the tested behavior is the local gateway/provider routing contract with a seeded Discord channel session.

- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

The focused RED test on the old behavior failed with `AssertionError: expected 'ollama' to be 'anthropic'`, showing ingress selection used the default model instead of the channel model.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `agentCommandFromIngress` started from `resolveDefaultModelForAgent()` and then applied stored/explicit overrides, but did not consider the existing `channels.modelByChannel` candidate for the session/channel context.
- Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered direct user/session overrides but not the ingress path where a channel default should seed the initial provider/model.
- Contributing context (if known): neighboring status/config paths already knew about `modelByChannel`, which made the runtime selection gap easy to miss.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/agent.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: ingress runs use `modelByChannel` when no stored/explicit override exists; configured aliases resolve correctly; stored and explicit overrides still win; auto-fallback primary probes use the channel primary.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the command ingress selection point directly while preserving existing override precedence contracts.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for the channel ingress selection gap.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Channel sessions with `channels.modelByChannel` now use that configured channel model for ingress/requester agent runs unless a stored session override or explicit one-off override is present.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
channel ingress -> agent default model -> stored/explicit overrides

After:
channel ingress -> agent default model -> channel model candidate -> stored/explicit overrides -> selected provider/model
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local checkout, Node v22.22.1
- Model/provider: local deterministic provider endpoints for the `openai/gpt-default` and `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6` provider-id paths
- Integration/channel (if any): seeded Discord channel session
- Relevant config (redacted): `channels.modelByChannel.discord.1489110116304424990 = anthropic/claude-opus-4-6`, global default `openai/gpt-default`

### Steps

1. Seed a Discord channel session in a temporary session store.
2. Start `openclaw gateway run` with temporary config/state and local provider endpoints.
3. Run `gateway call agent` for the seeded session and inspect provider request counts plus `agentMeta.provider/model`.

### Expected

- The channel-selected provider/model is used.
- The global default provider endpoint is not contacted.

### Actual

- `anthropic_requests=1`, `openai_default_requests=0`.
- `agentMeta.provider=anthropic`, `agentMeta.model=claude-opus-4-6`.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - focused regression subset: modelByChannel alias, auto-fallback, stored precedence, explicit precedence
  - full `src/commands/agent.test.ts`: 27 passed
  - post-fix runtime proof: channel provider selected, default provider not contacted
- Edge cases checked:
  - configured model aliases resolve for channel entries
  - stored session model overrides still beat channel defaults
  - explicit one-off run overrides still beat channel defaults
  - auto-fallback primary probe uses the channel primary
- What you did **not** verify:
  - live Discord API delivery
  - paid external provider APIs
  - full repository CI locally

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No existing PR review conversations yet.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: channel defaults could accidentally override stronger per-session or one-off user choices.
  - Mitigation: tests lock stored and explicit override precedence ahead of channel defaults.
- Risk: bare channel model aliases could bypass canonical model selection behavior.
  - Mitigation: channel entries resolve through the shared alias-aware resolver, with a regression test for a configured alias.
- Risk: once ingress runs honor `modelByChannel`, existing sessions can expose a separate auth/session-state edge when a stored `authProfileOverride` belongs to a different provider than the channel-selected model.
  - Mitigation: this PR keeps the routing fix focused and does not broaden auth-profile persistence policy. A narrow follow-up patch with regression coverage is prepared to handle that route-local persistence edge separately, so reviewers can evaluate the documented channel-routing fix independently.

target.title: Add native Telegram draft streaming support
target.body:
## Summary

- Add native Telegram `sendMessageDraft` streaming for DM answer text, producing smooth typing animation instead of choppy `editMessageText` edits
- Gated behind `channels.telegram.streaming.nativeTransport: true` + `mode: "partial"` + private chat scope; groups always fall back to edit-based streaming
- Extract shared draft utilities (`resolveSendMessageDraftApi`, `allocateTelegramDraftId`, `normalizeDraftText`) into `native-draft-api.ts`, reused by both tool-progress and answer draft modules

## How it works

When `nativeTransport` is enabled, DM answer streaming uses `sendMessageDraft` with a unique `draftId` for live preview updates. On finalization, `sendMessage` persists the final text. If `sendMessageDraft` is unavailable on the API client (older grammY or unsupported Bot API), falls back silently to the existing `sendMessage + editMessageText` path.

Edge cases addressed (these caused the previous maintainer note against reintroducing draft streaming):
- **30s draft expiry**: throttle loop fires every ~1s; tool calls pause answer draft and use a separate `draftId`
- **Duplicate finals**: `streamState.final` + `lane.finalized` flags prevent double materialization; `sendMessage` in `stop()` is the exclusive final path
- **Draft→final flash**: `sendMessage` fires immediately after last draft; draft expires naturally
- **>4096 chars**: draft truncated during streaming; full text delivered via `sendMessage`
- **Media/buttons**: text finalizes via stream, media sends separately (existing lane deliverer pattern)
- **Quote replies**: already skip preview streaming when `hasTelegramQuoteReply` is true

## Test plan

- [x] `native-draft-api.test.ts` — 8 tests: API resolution, draft ID allocation, text normalization
- [x] `native-answer-draft.test.ts` — 15 tests: unavailable API fallback, draft updates, stop/materialize lifecycle, ephemeral clear, forceNewMessage, >4096 truncation, error recovery, thread/reply params, dedup, minInitialChars debounce, renderText parse mode
- [x] `native-tool-progress-draft.test.ts` — 3 tests pass (regression check after shared utility extraction)
- [x] `config-schema.test.ts` — 41 tests pass (schema accepts `nativeTransport`)
- [x] `draft-stream.test.ts` — 32 tests pass (no regression to edit-based streaming)
- [x] `bot-message-dispatch.test.ts` — 80 tests pass (dispatch wiring)
- [x] Full Telegram extension suite — 1826 tests across 122 files, zero failures
- [x] Real Telegram proof: DM streaming with `nativeTransport: true` on bot-to-bot QA lane

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Native sendMessageDraft answer streaming in Telegram DMs
- **Real environment tested**: Live Telegram DM + group with test bot (@TestStream123123Bot), dev build from PR branch on macOS arm64 (M-series Mac mini, Node v24.14.1)
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Cloned repo, checked out PR branch, ran `pnpm install && pnpm build`, patched `openclaw.plugin.json` for schema gap (see bug note below), created isolated test config with `nativeTransport: true/false`, started dev gateway on port 18790 with separate `OPENCLAW_HOME`, tested in private DM and group chat with screen recordings
- **Evidence after fix**: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c38f7bad-6f19-435c-a980-9194b7127ec6
- **Observed result after fix**: Smooth native typing animation via `sendMessageDraft` in DM; choppy `editMessageText` edits in group chat and when `nativeTransport: false`, confirming both the toggle and group fallback work correctly. Single final `sendMessage` delivery — no duplicate messages observed.
- **What was not tested**: >4096 char overflow, 30s draft expiry during long tool calls

### Detailed test recordings

**Test 1: nativeTransport: false (DM)**
- Config: `streaming.nativeTransport: false`, `mode: "partial"`
- Result: Choppy edit-based streaming (editMessageText)
- Evidence: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ac999509-918e-44f7-8e3d-bf54dc580bdd

**Test 2: Group chat (nativeTransport: true)**
- Config: `streaming.nativeTransport: true`, `mode: "partial"`, group chat
- Result: Falls back to edit-based streaming (sendMessageDraft is DM-only)
- Evidence: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/72be8789-dbd4-4ef3-9f65-7534d551a40d

**Test 3: nativeTransport: true (DM)**
- Config: `streaming.nativeTransport: true`, `mode: "partial"`
- Result: Smooth native typing animation via sendMessageDraft
- Evidence: https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c38f7bad-6f19-435c-a980-9194b7127ec6

### Bug found during testing
`openclaw.plugin.json` missing `nativeTransport` in `channelConfigs.telegram.schema.streaming.properties`. The Zod schema correctly includes it but the generated plugin JSON schema metadata does not. Causes `must NOT have additional properties` on startup. Needs fix in metadata generation.

target.title: fix: add post-write verification to prevent false success reports
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #67136

The write tool was reporting "Successfully wrote X bytes" even when the file was not actually created on disk. This was happening because the upstream pi-coding-agent library reports success after calling the writeFile operation without verifying the file exists.

## Changes

This fix adds post-write verification to all write operations in OpenClaw:

### 1. Sandbox write operations (`createSandboxWriteOperations`)
- After `bridge.writeFile()`, verify file exists via `bridge.stat()`
- Check that the path is a file (not directory)
- Verify the file size matches expected bytes

### 2. Sandbox edit operations (`createSandboxEditOperations`)
- Same verification for the writeFile operation

### 3. Host write operations (`writeHostFile`)
- After `fs.writeFile()`, verify file exists via `fs.stat()`
- Check that the path is a file
- Verify the file size matches expected bytes

## Behavior

If any verification fails, an error is thrown with a clear message indicating what failed:
- `Write verification failed: file does not exist after write (path)`
- `Write verification failed: path is not a file after write (path)`
- `Write verification failed: expected X bytes but file has Y bytes (path)`

This ensures users are never told a write succeeded when it actually failed.

## Note

Host writes with `workspaceOnly=true` already had verification via `writeFileWithinRoot` which calls `verifyAtomicWriteResult`.

## Testing

Added tests in `src/agents/pi-tools.write-verification.test.ts` covering:
- Successful write with correct verification
- Nested directory creation
- Sandbox: file doesn't exist after write (returns null from stat)
- Sandbox: wrong type after write (directory instead of file)
- Sandbox: wrong size after write
- Issue #67136 specific scenario: writeFile succeeds but file doesn't exist

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** The write tool reported "Successfully wrote N bytes" while the target file never landed on disk (#67136). After this PR, a stat+size verifier runs immediately after every host and sandbox write/edit, so the user-visible result is now either a verified success or a clear `WriteVerificationError` describing what went wrong.

**Real environment tested:** Host write/edit path on macOS 25.3.0 / arm64, Node 25.9.0, exercising the same `src/agents/pi-tools.write-verification.ts` module that ships in this PR (no rebuilt copy, no mocks of the verifier). The reproducer is committed at `docs/proofs/67136/repro.mjs` and writes to real `os.tmpdir()` paths.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```
git checkout fix/67136-write-tool-false-success
node docs/proofs/67136/repro.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** Live terminal capture of `node docs/proofs/67136/repro.mjs` against the head of this branch — script exits 0 only if the pre-fix symptom reproduces and every post-fix verifier path fires on a real on-disk write. Full capture committed at `docs/proofs/67136/repro-output.txt`.

```
OpenClaw #67136 / PR #67202 — Real behaviour repro
node v25.9.0 | platform=darwin arm64

━━━ Scenario A: pre-fix behaviour — silent write success (the bug) ━━━
» tmpdir: /tmp/openclaw-proof-67136-A-2JNMO8
» target: /tmp/openclaw-proof-67136-A-2JNMO8/feature.md
agent → Successfully wrote 40 bytes to /tmp/openclaw-proof-67136-A-2JNMO8/feature.md
✗ Reality: file does NOT exist on disk. The agent's success message was a lie. This is #67136 reproduced.

━━━ Scenario B: post-fix behaviour — verifyHostFile catches the bug ━━━
» tmpdir: /tmp/openclaw-proof-67136-B-k7LRRc
✓ B1 normal write — verifyHostFile() OK for /tmp/openclaw-proof-67136-B-k7LRRc/ok.txt
✓ B2 verifier threw WriteVerificationError as expected:
      Write verification failed: file does not exist after write (/tmp/openclaw-proof-67136-B-k7LRRc/missing.txt)
✓ B3 verifier threw on size mismatch as expected:
      Write verification failed: expected 12 bytes but file has 5 bytes (/tmp/openclaw-proof-67136-B-k7LRRc/partial.txt)

━━━ Scenario C: post-fix behaviour — verifyWrittenStat catches sandbox-shape failures ━━━
✓ C1 stat=null → Write verification failed: file does not exist after write (/sandbox/missing)
✓ C2 stat={type:"directory"} → Write verification failed: path is not a file after write (/sandbox/feature)
✓ C3 stat={size:5} → Write verification failed: expected 11 bytes but file has 5 bytes (/sandbox/feature.md)
✓ C4 happy path — verifyWrittenStat() returned cleanly for matching stat.

━━━ Scenario D: edit-tool recovery must rethrow WriteVerificationError ━━━
✓ Recovery wrapper rethrew WriteVerificationError as required:
      Write verification failed: file does not exist after write (/tmp/openclaw-proof-67136-D-iJD46X/edit-target.txt)

━━━ Summary ━━━
  • A pre-fix silent failure → bug-reproduced
  • B1 normal write → pass
  • B2 file missing after write → pass
  • B3 size mismatch → pass
  • C1 sandbox stat=null → pass
  • C2 sandbox wrong type → pass
  • C3 sandbox wrong size → pass
  • C4 sandbox happy path → pass
  • D recovery rethrow → pass

PASS — Bug reproduced (pre-fix) and verifier caught every failure mode (post-fix).
```

**Observed result after fix:** The pre-fix scenario reproduces the reported false success verbatim (`Successfully wrote 40 bytes` for a file that never lands), and every post-fix scenario surfaces the new error string (`Write verification failed: …`) instead of a fake success. The host-edit recovery wrapper rethrows `WriteVerificationError` instead of converting it into a `Successfully replaced …` string, closing the related edit-path false-success vector. Exit code of the repro is 0 (PASS) on this branch and would be non-zero on `main`.

**What was not tested:** Did not live-replay on Ubuntu 24.04 with DeepSeek (the original reporter's stack from #67136) because that requires reporter-side credentials and a DeepSeek session; the symptom is reproduced at the `WriteOperations.writeFile` contract layer which is platform-agnostic, and the verifier is exercised on real host filesystem calls in scenarios B/C/D above.

target.title: Add experimental Evennia channel plugin
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an experimental Evennia channel plugin for OpenClaw-managed MUD characters.

The plugin provides:
- `evennia` and `evennia-staging` channel registrations
- multi-account routing for character connections
- an `evennia_command` tool for one-command-at-a-time in-world actions
- WebSocket/webclient login transport for Evennia
- in-room mention/direct-message dispatch into OpenClaw turns
- automatic `look` and cached `help` context for agent turns
- per-room/per-direct history windows feeding `InboundHistory` with at least 20 prior messages
- slow local/Ollama turn hardening via explicit no-run-timeout and longer block reply delivery timeout
- in-world status poses for ack/progress/error reactions

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/evennia/src/channel.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `jq . extensions/evennia/openclaw.plugin.json`
- `openclaw config validate`
- `openclaw --profile staging-dumbledong config validate`
- Booted the staging-Dumbledong gateway on port `18889` using the copied extension and verified the Evennia plugin loads cleanly.
- Deployed the extension to Patrick, Scoob, and Dumbledong local OpenClaw installs and verified `openclaw status --deep` reports Evennia OK on Scoob and Dumbledong.

## Notes

This is intentionally generic plugin code. Dongeon-specific lore/config remains local runtime configuration, not in the public plugin.

target.title: Fix local embedding worker safety
target.body:
## Summary

- Isolate local memory embeddings in a worker process by default so native `node-llama-cpp` teardown failures do not take down the gateway.
- Keep local embedding configuration unchanged; this PR does not add a `memorySearch.local.gpu` key or a QMD CPU policy knob.
- Degrade safely on local worker failure, activate configured embedding fallback with a reindex, and keep FTS-only search available when no fallback is configured.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/embeddings.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-timeout.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.mistral-provider.test.ts src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/agents/memory-search.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-44202-local-embedding-safety.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-44202-local-embedding-safety.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/plugin-sdk-dts-44202-local-embedding-safety.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/tsdown-build.mjs`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/reference/memory-config.md`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Apple Silicon local embedding worker crash risk from native `node-llama-cpp` / Metal cleanup is isolated from the gateway process without adding a new public config value.
Real environment tested: macOS local worktree, Node 24.16.0, focused unit/type/build proof.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: see Verification above; also checked built `dist/memory-core-local-embedding-worker.js` and the bundled embeddings chunk resolve the worker sidecar, checked generated bundles contain no `gpuLayers`, `QMD_FORCE_CPU`, or local GPU policy names, and launched the built default sidecar path far enough to receive the expected missing-optional-dependency error from inside the worker in this checkout.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed, typechecks passed, build passed, docs MDX passed, artifact check confirmed the worker sidecar exists and close ack is not followed by child `process.disconnect`.
Observed result after fix: local embeddings default to worker isolation; configured fallback activates and reindexes after a search-time local worker crash; no fallback keeps keyword search available; `memorySearch.local.gpu` is rejected by config validation.
What was not tested: a live Apple Silicon Metal teardown crash, because the local repro path did not reproduce the native crash; full local embedding startup with real `node-llama-cpp`, because this checkout does not have that optional runtime package installed.

Fixes #44202

target.title: feat(agents): add resumable field to sessions_list tool output
target.body:
## Why

Fixes #64103. Orchestrator agents see `status: "failed"`, `"timeout"`, or `"done"` on a session row and conclude the session is permanently dead, spawning a duplicate instead of calling `sessions_send` to resume. Status reflects the last turn outcome, not session lifecycle — `"killed"` is the only terminal state.

`reactivateCompletedSubagentSession` confirms this: it checks `endedAt`, not `status`. `sessions_send` uses `interruptSessionRunIfActive` for running sessions, so even `"running"` is sendable.

## What changed

- `sessions-list-tool.ts` — adds `readSessionResumable()` returning `boolean | undefined`; exposes a `resumable` field on each session row (`true` for all statuses except `"killed"`; `undefined` for unrecognized status)
- `sessions-helpers.ts` — adds `resumable?: boolean` to `SessionListRow` type
- `tool-description-presets.ts` — updates `describeSessionsListTool()` so agents see the contract directly: resumable is true for all statuses except killed
- `sessions-list-tool.test.ts` — 6 cases: `running`/`done`/`failed`/`timeout` → `true`, `killed` → `false`, unrecognized → `undefined`
- `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/` — regenerated 6 snapshot files to reflect updated tool description

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Orchestrator agents treat `status` as session liveness and can spawn duplicate agents. `readSessionResumable` defines resumability from run status: `"killed"` is not resumable; `"running"`, `"done"`, `"failed"`, and `"timeout"` are resumable; unrecognized statuses return `undefined`.

**Real environment tested:** Node.js 22, running the `readSessionResumable` status-mapping logic from `sessions-list-tool.ts`.

**Exact steps or command run after fix:**
```sh
node --input-type=module << 'EOF'
function readSessionRunStatus(value) {
  return ['running','done','failed','killed','timeout'].includes(value) ? value : undefined;
}
function readSessionResumable(value) {
  const status = readSessionRunStatus(value);
  return status !== undefined ? status !== 'killed' : undefined;
}
for (const status of ['running','done','failed','killed','timeout','bogus']) {
  console.log(`status="${status}" → resumable: ${readSessionResumable(status)}`);
}
EOF
```

**Observed result after fix:** The status-to-resumable mapping matches the intended behavior: only `"killed"` is false, known non-killed statuses are true, and an unrecognized status is `undefined`.

**Evidence:**
```text
status="running" → resumable: true
status="done" → resumable: true
status="failed" → resumable: true
status="killed" → resumable: false
status="timeout" → resumable: true
status="bogus" → resumable: undefined
```

**What was not tested:** No live OpenClaw instance was tested; this proof covers the direct status-to-resumable mapping with Node.js execution.

target.title: feat: thread sessionKey/sessionId into ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext
target.body:
## Problem

OpenClaw never sets the `user` field in LiteLLM completion requests. This means all interactive sessions appear blank in LiteLLM spend logs — impossible to distinguish Telegram vs Discord traffic, or attribute cost spikes to a specific session/channel.

Example of the problem in LiteLLM `/spend/logs`:
```
end_user: ''   model: claude-sonnet-4-6   # is this Telegram? Discord? no way to know
end_user: ''   model: claude-sonnet-4-6
```

## Solution

Add `sessionKey` and `sessionId` to `ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext` so that provider plugins can forward session identity to downstream proxies.

With this change, a custom provider plugin (or a future built-in behavior) can inject `user: sessionKey` into the completion request body:

```typescript
// In a custom provider plugin:
prepareExtraParams(ctx) {
  if (ctx.sessionKey) {
    return { ...ctx.extraParams, user: ctx.sessionKey };
  }
}
```

This would produce attributable spend logs:
```
end_user: agent:main:telegram:direct:571820863   model: claude-sonnet-4-6
end_user: agent:main:discord:channel:1480539453  model: claude-sonnet-4-6
```

## Changes

- **`src/plugins/types.ts`**: Add `sessionKey?: string` and `sessionId?: string` to `ProviderPrepareExtraParamsContext` with JSDoc
- **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/extra-params.ts`**: Thread `sessionKey`/`sessionId` through `resolvePreparedExtraParams` and `applyExtraParamsToAgent`  
- **`src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`**: Pass `params.sessionKey` and `params.sessionId` at the `applyExtraParamsToAgent` call site

## Non-breaking

Both fields are optional. No existing behavior changes. Providers that don't use them are unaffected.

target.body: ## Summary - Fixes incorrect Primary Model selection when switching between agents in Agents > Overview. - Fixes partial / blank Primary Model dropdowns after refresh or first load by ensuring the model catalog is available on Agents Overview paths. - Adds targeted tests for both selection stability and catalog hydration behavior. - Keeps the fix scoped to the Agents Overview model-selection path. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #41344 - Related #46275 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: 1. The agent details subtree could reuse stale DOM/select state when switching agents, which caused the Primary Model select to drift toward the previously viewed agent's state. 2. Agents Overview depended on a shared model catalog that was not guaranteed to be loaded on refresh / first-load / overview-only paths, so the select could render before the catalog was ready and degrade into a partial or blank-looking list. - Missing detection / guardrail: - Existing coverage covered some selected-value behavior, but it did not cover model-catalog hydration timing or the refresh/overview path where the catalog starts empty and arrives later. - Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): - Prior work around #41344 / #46275 addressed the agent-switching selection issue. If none, write `None`. - Switching between agents no longer causes the Primary Model select to show the wrong agent's value. - On refresh / first entry to Agents Overview, the Primary Model select no longer degrades into a misleading partial list. - While the model catalog is still loading, the UI now shows a loading state instead of pretending only a small subset of options exists. - Once the catalog loads, the full option list appears without losing the current selection. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? Open Agents > Overview > Model Selection > Primary Model. 2. Switch between a default agent and a non-default agent. 3. Refresh the page and reopen the Primary Model select immediately. 4. Optionally wait for model-catalog hydration or switch away/back to Overview. ### Expected - The select reflects the correct explicit value for the current agent. - The inherit-default option text is stable. - Refresh / first-load does not show a misleading partial catalog. - Once the catalog is available, the full model list appears without losing the selected value. ### Actual - Before this fix, switching agents could retain stale select state from another agent. - Before this fix, refresh / first-load could show only fallback options (for example inherit/current) or appear blank until the catalog became available later. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ### UI Evidence #### Before After refresh / first load, the Primary Model dropdown could show only fallback entries or appear blank. #### After After the fix, the Primary Model dropdown restores the full option list correctly after refresh / first load, without losing the selected value. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - target.title: CLI: clarify gateway url override auth hints
target.body:
## Summary
- improve the gateway URL override auth error with source-specific follow-up hints
- tell CLI users they can remove `--url` when they meant the default local or SSH-tunneled gateway
- tell env override users to set `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN` or `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PASSWORD` alongside `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL`

## Why
OpenClaw already correctly rejects gateway URL overrides unless explicit credentials are provided, but the current error is still easy to stumble over in real Docker, VPS, and TUI flows.

This update keeps the same security behavior while making the failure more actionable:
- CLI `--url` users get a hint that they may not need `--url` at all for the default local or tunneled gateway
- `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_URL` users get a direct explanation that env URL overrides do not reuse config-only credentials and must be paired with explicit env auth

That makes the secure path easier to understand without loosening the override guard.

target.title: fix(cli): validate gateway-rpc --timeout
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Gateway-RPC based CLI commands accept `--timeout`, but invalid values (e.g. `--timeout nope`) get coerced to `NaN` and silently fall back to `10_000ms`.
- Why it matters: Users think they're overriding the timeout, but a typo can change behavior (and the fallback is inconsistent with the CLI default `30_000ms`).
- What changed: Validate `--timeout` in `callGatewayFromCli` and reject invalid values with a clear error; keep the intended default at `30s` when the option is missing.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to gateway request/transport semantics beyond CLI option validation; no refactors outside the CLI gateway-rpc helper.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens

target.title: Fix getSubagentDepth for legacy subagent session keys (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `getSubagentDepth()` returned `0` for legacy subagent session keys like `subagent:worker` because it only counted occurrences of the substring `":subagent:"`.
- Why it matters: Call sites that use depth as a fallback signal can misclassify subagent sessions as non-subagent when session-store metadata is missing, impacting announce/delivery and depth gating.
- What changed: `getSubagentDepth()` now counts `subagent:` segments at token boundaries (`(^|:)subagent:`) within the agent-scoped portion of the key.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to session key shapes, parsing rules, or any subagent spawn/store metadata.

target.title: Agents: add compaction modes (warn, error, none) with proactive conte…
target.body:
Summary

   - Problem: Users often lose important conversation history due to aggressive
     auto-compaction, or they encounter cryptic provider-specific "context
     window exceeded" errors when the window is full.
   - Why it matters: Advanced users need explicit control over context
     management to prevent silent history loss and to receive clear, actionable
     warnings before a session fails.
   - What changed: Added three new compaction modes (warn, error, none) backed
     by a proactive context guard that estimates tokens before each LLM call.
     Integrated Greptile feedback to ensure the guard is robust even if token
     estimation fails.
   - What did NOT change (scope boundary): The existing logic for default and
     safeguard modes remains untouched.

  Change Type (select all)

   - [ ] Bug fix
   - [x] Feature
   - [ ] Refactor required for the fix

   - [ ] Chore/infra

   - [ ] Memory / storage
   - [ ] Integrations
   - [ ] API / contracts
   - [ ] UI / DX
   - [ ] CI/CD / infra

  Linked Issue/PR

   - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

  Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

   - Root cause: N/A (New feature)

  Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

   - Coverage level that should have caught this:
     - [x] Unit test (Verified via pnpm check and oxlint)
   - Target test or file: src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts
   - Scenario the test should lock in: Ensuring warn mode returns the cleanup
     message and error mode throws before hitting the LLM.
   - If no new test is added, why not: Manual verification was performed in a
     live environment to ensure the user-visible messages appear correctly in
     the messaging channel.

  User-visible / Behavior Changes

   - New configuration options in agents.defaults.compaction.mode: "warn",
     "error", and "none".
   - Users in warn mode will see a message: 🧹 Context near limit, use /compact
     instead of the agent failing or compacting automatically.

  Security Impact (required)

   - New permissions/capabilities? No
   - Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
   - New/changed network calls? No
   - Command/tool execution surface changed? No
   - Data access scope changed? No

  Repro + Verification

  Environment

   - OS: macOS (darwin)
   - Runtime/container: Node.js 22.22.1
   - Model/provider: Verified with OpenAI/Anthropic providers.

  Steps

   1. Set agents.defaults.compaction.mode to "warn".
   2. Set a high reserveTokens (e.g., 100,000) to trigger the guard immediately.
   3. Send a message to the agent.

  Expected

   - The agent stops and returns: 🧹 Context near limit, use /compact.

  Actual

   - The agent correctly identifies the "near limit" state and returns the
     warning without calling the LLM.

  Evidence

   - [x] Trace/log snippets: Verified [compaction-guard] context near limit
     (mode=warn) logs in gateway.log.

  Human Verification (required)

   - Verified scenarios: warn mode (message returned), error mode (error
     thrown), and none mode (auto-compaction disabled).
   - Edge cases checked: Integrated Greptile feedback to handle estimateTokens
     failures; error mode now re-throws with cause, and warn mode provides a
     fallback warning instead of failing open.
   - What you did not verify: Latency impact on extremely large (1M+ tokens)
     histories, though estimateTokens is generally fast.

  Review Conversations

   - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in
     this PR.
   - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or
     maintainer judgment.

   - Config/env changes? Yes (New optional config fields).
   - Migration needed? No.

     "default" or "safeguard".
   - Files/config to restore: ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json.

  Risks and Mitigations

   - Risk: Token estimation failure could block the agent.

       falls back to a safe warning message; in error mode, it provides a
       descriptive error to the user.
