target.title: fix(gateway): tolerate transient pre-hello closes in CLI calls
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: One-shot gateway calls from the CLI (for example `openclaw cron status` / `openclaw cron list --json`) can fail with `gateway closed (1000):` when the first WebSocket connection closes with code `1000` and an empty reason *before* `hello-ok`, even though the client would immediately reconnect and complete the handshake successfully.
- Why it matters: This surfaces as a flaky but user-visible failure path for CLI commands while the gateway itself is healthy, which breaks automation and undermines trust in the CLI.
- What changed: `executeGatewayRequestWithScopes(...)` now tracks whether `hello-ok` has been seen and ignores only a **pre-hello** close with code `1000` and an empty reason; `GatewayClient.connect` treats that same transient connect error as non-fatal so the existing reconnect logic can complete; added focused tests covering both the one-shot call path and the client reconnect behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to auth/secret handling, no changes to other close codes or non-empty close reasons, no changes to broader reconnect policy, and no config or CLI surface changes.

*(AI-assisted: drafted and iterated with an OpenClaw workspace agent; changes and tests were reviewed and verified by a human.)*

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: N/A (fixing a locally diagnosed CLI gateway handshake failure that did not yet have a public issue)
- Related: internal debugging of transient pre-hello `1000` closes on CLI gateway calls
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: The one-shot gateway request wrapper (`executeGatewayRequestWithScopes`) treated any `onClose` event as terminal, even if it happened *before* `hello-ok`. In some environments, the underlying WebSocket stack can emit a transient `1000` close with an empty reason during the initial handshake, while `GatewayClient` reconnects and later completes `hello` successfully.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no distinction between a transient pre-hello close that can be recovered by reconnect vs. a final close after a completed hello. The wrapper did not track whether `hello-ok` had been seen and did not special-case this transient `1000` path.
- Prior context: This shows up as flaky failures on CLI commands like `openclaw cron status`/`list` with `gateway closed (1000):` even though the gateway is reachable and the Control UI can talk to it.
- Why this regressed now: This is a behavior interaction between the CLI wrapper and gateway/WebSocket behavior that surfaced more often in certain deployments; it is not tied to a single recent refactor, but to the lack of pre-hello vs post-hello distinction in the close handling.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: Verified that the gateway itself was healthy (Control UI worked, direct WebSocket loopback was fine) and that the failure was in the CLI handshake path, not gateway liveness.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/gateway/call.test.ts`: new test ensuring `callGateway({ method: "health" })` waits through a transient pre-hello `1000` close and resolves successfully once `hello-ok` arrives.
  - `src/gateway/client.test.ts`: new test ensuring `GatewayClient` suppresses the spurious `gateway connect failed: ... (1000)` log + `onConnectError` callback when a pre-hello `1000` close is followed by a successful reconnect and `hello-ok`.
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - A CLI one-shot gateway call that sees a transient pre-hello `1000` + empty-reason close should not fail early; it should wait for the reconnect and complete normally.
  - The same transient close should not be logged as a connect-failed error or reported to `onConnectError` when the reconnect ultimately succeeds.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  - The bug is localized to the gateway call wrapper and `GatewayClient` connect path. Unit-level tests against these two entry points are sufficient to prevent future regressions in this exact transient-close path without requiring heavier end-to-end scenarios.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A (tests added).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- CLI calls that go through `callGateway`/`executeGatewayRequestWithScopes` are more robust against a specific transient handshake pattern: a pre-hello WebSocket close with code `1000` and an empty reason.
- Commands like `openclaw cron status` and `openclaw cron list --json` should no longer fail sporadically with `gateway closed (1000):` on otherwise healthy gateways.
- No change to error behavior for other close codes, non-empty close reasons, auth failures, or timeouts.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) **No**
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) **No**
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) **No**

If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (container/VM environment)
- Runtime/container: Node 22, pnpm-managed workspace
- Model/provider: N/A (gateway + CLI path only)
- Integration/channel (if any): CLI (`openclaw cron`)
- Relevant config (redacted): Gateway reachable on local loopback, CLI configured to talk to the local gateway.

### Steps

1. Start a local gateway and configure the CLI against it.
2. On a build without this fix, run `openclaw cron status` or `openclaw cron list --json` repeatedly in an environment where the WebSocket stack occasionally emits a transient pre-hello `1000` + empty-reason close.
3. Observe intermittent failures with `gateway closed (1000):` even though the Control UI and direct WebSocket loopback remain healthy.
4. Apply this change and re-run the same CLI commands.

### Expected

- CLI commands should tolerate the transient pre-hello `1000` + empty-reason close, wait for the reconnect, and succeed normally when the gateway is healthy.

### Actual

- Before: CLI calls can fail immediately with `gateway closed (1000):` on the first handshake, despite the gateway being healthy and reconnects succeeding under the hood.
- After: The same commands complete successfully; the transient close is no longer reported as a fatal error, and the new unit tests cover this behavior.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (simulated via new unit tests that reproduce the pre-hello `1000` close pattern and confirm the corrected behavior)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/call.test.ts src/gateway/client.test.ts` on the patched branch.
  - Manual verification on a local install that previously exhibited this bug: `openclaw cron status` and `openclaw cron list --json` no longer fail with `gateway closed (1000):` when the gateway is healthy.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Only the specific `pre-hello + code 1000 + empty reason` path is suppressed; other close codes or non-empty reasons still fail the call as before.
  - Timeouts still behave as before; this change does not alter timeout behavior.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` for the entire monorepo; this PR focuses on gateway client + call behavior and is covered by targeted gateway tests.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. *(No prior bot conversations for this PR; marked proactively.)*

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) **Yes**
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) **No**
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) **No**

If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - Revert commit `fbe47e1874` (or the PR merge commit) to restore the prior `executeGatewayRequestWithScopes` and `GatewayClient.connect` behavior.
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/gateway/call.ts`
  - `src/gateway/client.ts`
  - `src/gateway/call.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/client.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - If a real, unrecoverable pre-hello `1000` close starts occurring in the wild, CLI callers might now see a timeout instead of an immediate `gateway closed (1000):` error for that specific pattern.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk:
  - Misclassifying a non-recoverable pre-hello `1000` + empty-reason close as transient, causing the CLI to wait for a reconnect that never meaningfully succeeds and eventually hit a timeout instead of failing immediately.
  - Mitigation: The suppression is narrowly scoped to `pre-hello + code 1000 + empty reason`. Any close with a reason, any non-`1000` code, or any close after `hello-ok` still fails the call as before. Timeouts are unchanged, so a genuinely broken gateway still propagates as a failure rather than silently succeeding.
- Risk:
  - Future changes to error message formatting could break the `GatewayClient.connect` string match for the transient `gateway closed (1000):` error.
  - Mitigation: The new unit tests in `src/gateway/client.test.ts` exercise this behavior explicitly; if the error message shape changes in the future, the tests should fail and force an update to the matching logic or a move to more structured error signaling.

target.title: fix(acp): add system_event stream relay to parent session for ACP spawn
target.body:
## 🐛 Problem

When using `sessions_spawn` to create an ACP session with `streamTo: "parent"`, Codex`s `system_event` type events (such as clarifying questions and progress updates) are not being relayed to the parent session. This prevents users from receiving timely clarifying questions from Codex during interactive programming tasks.

### Impact

- Users do not receive Codex`s clarifying questions (e.g., "Which number format do you prefer: integers only or decimals?")
- Progress updates from Codex are not shown to users
- Only `assistant_delta` (character-by-character output) and `lifecycle` (start/end) events are relayed
- Forces users to manually check logs or ask for status, breaking the interactive flow

## 🔍 Root Cause

In the `startAcpSpawnParentStreamRelay` function`s event listener, only two event streams are handled:

```typescript
if (event.stream === "assistant") {
    // ✅ Handles assistant_delta (character-by-character output)
    ...
}
if (event.stream !== "lifecycle") return;  // ❌ system_event is filtered out here!
// Handles lifecycle events (start/end/error)
```

**`system_event` events are silently ignored**, even though they are properly logged to the stream file.

## 📋 Reproduction

### Minimal Example

```typescript
// Spawn an ACP task with streamTo: "parent"
const result = await sessions_spawn({
  runtime: "acp",
  agentId: "codex",
  task: "Please help me write a calculator with basic arithmetic operations",
  streamTo: "parent",  // Enable streaming to parent session
  mode: "run",
  timeoutSeconds: 300
});
```

### Expected Behavior

When Codex asks a clarifying question:
```
System: [timestamp] codex: Please confirm: Which number format do you prefer?
  1. Integers only (e.g., 1, -2)
  2. Support decimals (e.g., 1.5, -0.75)
```

### Actual Behavior

- ❌ User receives **no notification**
- ✅ Log file correctly records the event:
  ```json
  {"kind":"system_event","contextKey":"acp-spawn:...:progress","text":"codex: Please confirm: Which number format do you prefer?..."}
  ```

## 🔬 Investigation

### Log Analysis

From `/Users/liuestc/.openclaw/agents/codex/sessions/<sessionId>.acp-stream.jsonl`:

```json
// ✅ Properly logged
{"kind":"system_event","contextKey":"acp-spawn:ad16d566:...:progress","text":"codex: ACP test successful! Current time: 1774440503"}
{"kind":"system_event","contextKey":"acp-spawn:ad16d566:...:done","text":"codex run completed."}

// ❌ But not relayed to parent session
```

### Code Analysis

Location: `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts` → `startAcpSpawnParentStreamRelay()`

Current event handling:

| Event Stream | Logged to File | Relayed to Parent |
|-------------|----------------|-------------------|
| `assistant` | ✅ | ✅ |
| `lifecycle` | ✅ | ✅ |
| `system_event` | ✅ | ❌ **BUG** |

## ✅ Solution

Add handling for `system_event` stream in the `onAgentEvent` listener:

```typescript
// NEW: Handle system_event stream
if (event.stream === "system_event") {
    const text = event.data?.text;
    if (text) {
        emit(text, event.contextKey);
    }
    return;
}
```

## 📁 Files Modified

- `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts` - Add `system_event` stream handling in `startAcpSpawnParentStreamRelay()`

## 🧪 Testing

### Test Case 1: Clarifying Question

1. Spawn ACP task with complex requirements
2. Codex asks clarifying question
3. **Verify**: User receives the question immediately via System message

### Test Case 2: Progress Update

1. Spawn ACP task with `streamTo: "parent"`
2. Codex outputs progress updates
3. **Verify**: User sees progress updates in real-time

### Test Case 3: Completion Notification

1. Spawn ACP task
2. Codex completes the task
3. **Verify**: User receives "codex run completed." notification

## 📊 Before vs After

| Scenario | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Clarifying question | ❌ Not shown | ✅ Shown immediately |
| Progress update | ❌ Not shown | ✅ Shown in real-time |
| Completion notice | ✅ Via lifecycle | ✅ Via system_event |
| Assistant output | ✅ Via assistant | ✅ Via assistant |

## 📝 Related

- Affects all users using ACP + Codex for interactive programming
- Particularly impactful for complex tasks requiring multi-turn clarification
- No breaking changes - only adds missing functionality

target.title: media: fix resource leaks and temp file cleanup in downloadToFile
target.body:
## Summary

- **Fix write stream leak on size-limit abort**: When a download exceeds `MAX_BYTES`, `req.destroy()` was called but the `createWriteStream` was never closed, leaking file descriptors under load
- **Add `res.on('error')` handler**: Response-level errors (premature close, decompression failures) could fire before `pipeline` wired up its own handler, causing unhandled rejections
- **Consume redirect/error response bodies**: Added `res.resume()` on redirect and error status paths to free the underlying socket instead of leaving it hanging
- **Guard against double-settle**: Added a `settled` flag in the Promise executor to prevent resolve/reject from being called multiple times across concurrent error paths
- **Clean up partial `.tmp` files**: `saveMediaSource` now removes the temp file in a `catch` block when `downloadToFile` fails, preventing orphaned partial downloads from accumulating on disk

## Test plan

- [x] Added test: temp file cleanup when download exceeds size limit
- [x] Added test: temp file cleanup on HTTP 500 error status
- [x] All 26 existing `store.test.ts` tests continue to pass
- [x] `pnpm tsgo` — zero type errors
- [x] `pnpm check` — all lint/format checks pass

target.title: fix(agents): add compaction event observability
target.body:
## Summary

This PR improves embedded compaction observability by exposing clearer start/end details for auto-compaction attempts.

## What changes

- include `reason` on compaction start events
- include `completed`, `hasResult`, `willRetry`, and sanitized `errorMessage` on compaction end events
- add focused tests around compaction event payloads

## Why

When compaction is triggered during overflow recovery, the current logs and events make it hard to distinguish:

- compaction never started
- compaction started but failed
- compaction produced no result
- compaction is about to retry
- compaction actually completed

This is especially painful in fallback / rate-limit scenarios, where operators may otherwise see repeated compaction attempts without enough signal to tell whether the system is making progress.

## Scope

This PR intentionally does **not** change:

- compaction UX / notification policy
- model fallback policy
- `/status` compactionCount reconciliation

Those are better handled separately and already have related upstream discussion.

target.title: fix(skill-creator): default to workspace skills directory to prevent data loss on upgrade
target.body:
## Problem

When using the `skill-creator` skill to automatically generate a new skill, skills were being created in the global `node_modules/openclaw/skills/` directory instead of the user's workspace directory (`~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/`).

This is a critical issue because:
1. Skills created in the global directory are lost when upgrading OpenClaw via `npm install -g openclaw@latest`
2. Users expect their custom skills to persist across upgrades
3. The global `node_modules` directory is not the appropriate location for user-created content

## Root Cause

The `init_skill.py` script required a `--path` argument, and the skill-creator SKILL.md documentation showed examples with relative paths like `skills/public`. When the agent executed this command, the relative path was resolved against the current working directory, which was often the global OpenClaw installation directory.

## Solution

1. **Make `--path` optional**: The `init_skill.py` script now defaults to the workspace skills directory when `--path` is not specified.

2. **Smart default path resolution**: Added `resolve_default_skills_dir()` function that:
   - Defaults to `~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/`
   - Respects the `OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR` environment variable if set

3. **Updated documentation**: Enhanced SKILL.md with:
   - Clear warning about NOT creating skills in global node_modules
   - Examples showing the new default behavior
   - Explicit guidance to use absolute paths for custom locations

## Changes

- `skills/skill-creator/scripts/init_skill.py`: Make `--path` optional, add default path resolution
- `skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md`: Add warnings and updated examples

## Testing

The changes maintain backward compatibility:
- Existing usage with `--path` continues to work as before
- New default behavior safely directs skills to the workspace directory
- Respects environment variable overrides for advanced configurations

target.title: fix(auto-reply): require operator.admin for /stop command
target.body:
Fixes a missing authorization check in the `/stop` command handler within the auto-reply module. The command previously relied on a base scope check (`rejectUnauthorizedCommand`) instead of verifying the required elevated scope.

## Root Cause
The `handleStopCommand` function invoked `rejectUnauthorizedCommand` to authorize requests. This does not enforce the `operator.admin` scope requirement necessary for mutating internal commands like session abort, potentially allowing unauthorized users to stop sessions.

## Fix
Replaced `rejectUnauthorizedCommand` with `requireGatewayClientScopeForInternalChannel` to explicitly enforce the `operator.admin` scope.

## Reference
Matches the fix pattern in PR #54097.

target.title: fix: Discord: avoid blocking on oversized inbound media
target.body:
AI-assisted: Yes
Testing: Targeted unit test only (`pnpm test -- src/media/read-response-with-limit.test.ts`)

## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: Oversized Discord inbound attachments could leave the affected inbound channel stuck after the download crossed the configured media size limit.
- Why it matters: After one large image, later messages in the same channel could stop being processed until the gateway was restarted.
- What changed: The shared bounded-response reader now cancels overflowed streams without awaiting `reader.cancel()`, a regression test locks in the non-returning cancel case, and the matching Matrix helper now uses the same non-blocking overflow cleanup.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not raise media limits, change attachment storage behavior, or change outbound media handling.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54112
- Related N/A
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: `readResponseWithLimit()` awaited `reader.cancel()` on max-bytes overflow. If the underlying stream did not resolve cancel promptly, the inbound job stayed pending.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No regression test covered an overflow path where `reader.cancel()` never resolves.
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): Discord inbound attachment downloads flow through `fetchRemoteMedia()` into the shared bounded-response reader. The same await-on-cancel pattern also existed in the Matrix SDK copy of that helper.
- Why this regressed now: Large Discord attachments are a common way to hit the overflow branch because Discord inbound media defaults to 8 MB in this path.
- If unknown, what was ruled out: Not a normal HTTP fetch failure path, not media persistence, and not the per-channel queue implementation itself.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should have caught this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/media/read-response-with-limit.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Reject immediately on overflow even if `reader.cancel()` never resolves.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The bug is in the shared overflow reader logic, so a focused unit test hits the exact stuck path without needing a live Discord setup.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- Oversized Discord inbound attachments now fail fast instead of potentially leaving the affected inbound channel unresponsive.
- No user-facing config or default changes.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Discord inbound attachments
- Relevant config (redacted): Default Discord inbound media limit (`channels.discord.mediaMaxMb = 8`)

### Steps

1. Send an image attachment larger than the configured Discord inbound media limit to a Discord channel where OpenClaw is active.
2. Let the inbound media download cross the overflow path in the bounded response reader.
3. Send another message in the same channel.

### Expected

- The oversized attachment should fail gracefully.
- The same channel should continue processing later inbound messages without a restart.

### Actual

- Before this change, the overflow path could leave the channel's inbound processing stuck until the gateway was restarted.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: Ran `pnpm test -- src/media/read-response-with-limit.test.ts`, including the new regression case where `cancel()` never resolves.
- Edge cases checked: In-limit reads, overflow reads, custom overflow errors, and idle timeout behavior.
- What you did **not** verify: I did not run a live end-to-end Discord repro against a real gateway session.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this PR or restore the previous overflow cancellation behavior in the bounded-response reader helpers.
- Files/config to restore: `src/media/read-response-with-limit.ts`, `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/read-response-with-limit.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Unexpected regressions in bounded media downloads or attachment reads that now abort earlier than expected.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: The shared Matrix helper was updated alongside the Discord path without a live Matrix repro.
  - Mitigation: The change is a direct equivalent of the shared fix, preserves overflow semantics, and only removes the blocking wait on cancel.
- Risk: This PR also removes two pre-existing unused imports that were already failing lint on `main`.
  - Mitigation: Those lines are behavior-free cleanup only and were required to let the repo checks pass for this branch.

target.title: feat(synology-chat): add group/channel support
target.body:
## Summary

- Add group/channel message support for Synology Chat alongside existing DMs
- Each channel is configured as a named account with its own webhook token, incoming URL, and dedicated webhookPath
- Group policy (`disabled` | `allowlist` | `open`) with per-channel overrides, following the Telegram/Discord pattern

## Changes

- Add `groupPolicy`, `groupAllowFrom`, and `channels` config fields to account resolution (default: `disabled`)
- Add `group-access` module with policy evaluation and mention resolution
- Route inbound messages by `channel_id` presence: group → `groupPolicy`, DM → `dmPolicy`
- Add `sendToChannel()` for channel replies (`method=incoming`, no `user_ids`)
- Add security warnings for `groupPolicy="open"` and empty `groupAllowFrom`
- Update docs: integration types (Bot vs Webhooks), group setup guide, per-channel overrides

## Synology Chat specifics

Synology Chat uses separate integrations for DMs (Bot) and channels (Outgoing + Incoming webhooks), each with a distinct token. This maps naturally to OpenClaw's multi-account model: one account per channel, each with its own `webhookPath`.

## Test plan

- [x] 124 unit tests pass (14 new group-access tests)
- [x] All pre-commit hooks pass (format, lint, TypeScript, boundaries)
- [x] Live tested: DM messages route and reply correctly
- [x] Live tested: channel messages route and reply in-channel
- [x] Backward compatible: existing DM-only configs work unchanged

target.title: acpx: add terminal-truth artifacts and strict terminal states
target.body:
## Summary

This PR is **Patch 1A only**.

Current PR head:
- `bb45eb5acb517c8f5755f816778fc0b4cbfb40fb`

Base:
- `17c1ee7716`

This PR currently consists of two commits:
- `bcb4116bc756bd6fe5f1fa6f3d5094c856130562` — `acpx: add terminal-truth artifacts and strict terminal states`
- `bb45eb5acb517c8f5755f816778fc0b4cbfb40fb` — `acpx: preserve terminal truth on route mirror failures`

This remains the terminal-truth-only split for ACP runtime-wrapped execution.

## Scope

Included in this PR:
- wrapper-owned per-run ACP artifacts
  - `route.json`
  - `terminal.json`
  - `stdout.log`
  - `stderr.log`
- strict terminal states
- durable completion gating based on persisted terminal truth
- raw stdout/stderr capture under wrapper ownership
- collision-safe persisted `run_id` allocation
- terminal-truth-focused tests
- abort-before-spawn fix inside Patch 1A
- narrow follow-up fixes for review feedback:
  - preserve terminal truth when `route.json` mirror write fails
  - retry run-id allocation only on `EEXIST`

Out of scope:
- **Patch 1B**
- Cursor-specific fallback behavior
- `shouldUseExecFallback(...)`
- `buildExecArgs(...)`
- `exec` vs `prompt` route switching
- `exec_oneshot`
- fallback-specific tests / mock exec fixture path

## Abort-before-spawn fix

This PR includes the narrow Patch 1A follow-up for the pre-spawn abort race:
- recheck abort after `createRunArtifacts(...)`
- do not spawn if abort was requested before spawn
- finalize already-created artifacts as `lost`
- keep `started_at` unset
- emit no new event

## Follow-up fixes

The review-driven follow-up commit adds two narrow fixes without changing Patch 1A scope:
- preserve already-persisted `terminal.json` truth when `route.json` mirror write fails
- treat `route.json` as a best-effort mirror so mirror failure does not trigger a later `lost` overwrite path
- constrain run-id allocation retry to `EEXIST` only
- rethrow non-`EEXIST` `mkdir` failures immediately

## Validation

Review-scope validation:
- `extensions/acpx/src/runtime-internals/run-artifacts.test.ts`
- `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.test.ts`
- result: **31/31 passed**
- `build:strict-smoke`: **passed**

## Durability note

Patch 1A provides a much stronger durability contract, but not a perfectly atomic one in every crash window.
`terminal.json` should be treated as the strongest source of terminal truth, while `route.json` is a mirrored/secondary record and can theoretically lag in crash windows.

## Notes

This PR should be reviewed as **Patch 1A only**.
Patch 1B remains separate and out of scope.

target.body:
## Summary

- Adds [Defuddle](https://github.com/kepano/defuddle) as the primary content extraction engine for `web_fetch` `text/html` responses, with `@mozilla/readability` retained as a fallback when Defuddle returns no content.
- Refactors `extractReadableContent` into two focused helpers (`extractWithDefuddle` + `extractWithReadability`) orchestrated by a single public entry point that tries Defuddle first, then Readability, and tags the result with the extractor used.
- Lazy-loads `defuddle/node` on first use (same pattern as the existing Readability loader) to keep startup cost zero.

## Motivation

Defuddle ([announcement](https://x.com/kepano/status/2034374124269940898)) provides cleaner article extraction for a wider range of sites than Readability alone. By trying it first and falling back to Readability, we get better default extraction without losing coverage on pages where Readability still does better.

## Changes

| Area | What changed |
|---|---|
| **Runtime** | `src/agents/tools/web-fetch-utils.ts` — new `loadDefuddleDeps()` loader, `extractWithDefuddle()`, refactored `extractWithReadability()`, updated `extractReadableContent()` orchestrator |
| **Tool wiring** | `src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ts` — extractor tag comes from result; error messages updated |
| **Config / schema** | `types.tools.ts`, `schema.help.ts`, `schema.labels.ts`, `schema.base.generated.ts` — label/help text now says "Defuddle (with Readability fallback)" |
| **Docs** | `docs/tools/web-fetch.md`, `docs/reference/api-usage-costs.md` — pipeline description updated |
| **Generated baselines** | `docs/.generated/config-baseline.json`, `.jsonl` — regenerated |
| **Tests** | Renamed `web-tools.readability.test.ts` → `web-tools.defuddle.test.ts`; updated `web-fetch.cf-markdown.test.ts` and `web-tools.fetch.test.ts` assertions |
| **Scripts** | Renamed `readability-basic-compare.ts` → `defuddle-basic-compare.ts` |
| **Deps** | Added `defuddle ^0.14.0`; `@mozilla/readability` retained for fallback |

## Backward compatibility

- The config key `tools.web.fetch.readability` is unchanged (still a boolean toggle). Existing user configs continue to work as-is.
- Readability fallback means sites that Defuddle doesn't handle well still get extracted.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm check` passes (lint, format, type-check, all boundary checks)
- [ ] `pnpm test -- src/agents/tools/web-tools.defuddle.test.ts` — Defuddle extracts article text and markdown
- [ ] `pnpm test -- src/agents/tools/web-fetch.cf-markdown.test.ts` — CF markdown falls back to defuddle/readability
- [ ] `pnpm test -- src/agents/tools/web-tools.fetch.test.ts` — extraction fallback chain and error messages

target.title: Web UI: add heartbeat stop button and fix progress updates
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a pulsing heart button to the webchat toolbar that appears when heartbeats are enabled or a task is actively running — clicking it aborts the active run and disables heartbeats via the `set-heartbeats` API
- Fix the agent silently ignoring user requests for milestone/progress updates by adding explicit system prompt instructions that make inline status updates mandatory for iterative tasks and guide the agent to create temp cron jobs for time-based periodic checks

## Changes

### Webchat heartbeat stop button
- **`ui/src/ui/icons.ts`** — Add `heartPulse` icon (Lucide heart-pulse SVG)
- **`ui/src/ui/app.ts`** / **`app-view-state.ts`** — Add `heartbeatsEnabled` and `heartbeatStopping` reactive state
- **`ui/src/ui/app-gateway.ts`** — Derive `heartbeatsEnabled` from `HealthSummary.heartbeatSeconds` on connect; update on `heartbeat` broadcast events
- **`ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts`** — Add heartbeat props to `ChatProps`; render pulsing heart button when heartbeats are enabled or task is busy
- **`ui/src/ui/app-render.ts`** — Wire button to abort active run + call `set-heartbeats` API
- **`ui/src/styles/chat/layout.css`** — Danger-colored icon with pulse animation

### Progress update delivery fix
- **`src/agents/system-prompt.ts`** — Add "Progress Updates & Milestone Reporting" section with:
  - Mandatory inline updates for sequential/iterative work (output status after each item)
  - Cron-based scheduled updates for long-running/background tasks
  - Explicit trigger phrase matching ("give me updates", "status update", "milestone", etc.)
  - Updated cron tool description to mention milestone tracking

## Test plan
- [ ] Start gateway, open webchat UI, verify heart button appears when heartbeats are enabled
- [ ] Send a long-running task, verify heart button appears during active run
- [ ] Click heart button, verify task aborts and heartbeats disable
- [ ] Send iterative task with "give me a status update for each", verify agent outputs inline updates between steps
- [ ] Request time-based updates ("check every 5 minutes"), verify agent creates cron job

target.title: fix(scripts): avoid mutating tracked auth-monitor template during setup
target.body:
## Summary
- keep the tracked `openclaw-auth-monitor.service` template unchanged during setup
- render the auth-monitor user service in a temporary file before installing it
- render `ExecStart` to the current checkout's `scripts/auth-monitor.sh`
- replace `NOTIFY_*` environment lines by key so reruns repair old locally-mutated templates
- avoid adding a `python3` runtime prerequisite to this setup path

## Problem
`scripts/setup-auth-system.sh` currently edits the tracked systemd template in-place before copying it into `~/.config/systemd/user/`.

That dirties the repository working tree with machine-local values and makes the generated user unit inherit the template's hardcoded `/home/admin/openclaw/...` path instead of the current checkout.

## Root cause
The setup flow applies substitutions directly to the repository template file:

- `scripts/systemd/openclaw-auth-monitor.service`

and only copies it afterwards.

## What changed
- copy the auth-monitor service template into a temporary materialized unit
- validate and render `ExecStart` and notification environment lines before installing the unit
- atomically move the rendered service into `~/.config/systemd/user/` only after rendering succeeds
- copy the timer only after the service unit has been rendered successfully
- match existing `Environment=NOTIFY_*=` lines by key, including values left by older setup runs
- render with Bash builtins instead of adding a `python3` dependency

## Before / After

### Before
- setup edited `scripts/systemd/openclaw-auth-monitor.service` in-place
- running setup dirtied the repository working tree with machine-local values
- the generated user unit inherited the template's hardcoded `/home/admin/openclaw/...` path
- a missing renderer or placeholder mismatch could overwrite an existing service with the raw template

### After
- setup renders a temporary materialized service and installs it only after validation passes
- the tracked template remains unchanged
- the generated `ExecStart` points to the current checkout's `scripts/auth-monitor.sh`
- reruns repair both default commented placeholders and previously rendered `NOTIFY_*` lines

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed:** `scripts/setup-auth-system.sh` renders and installs the auth-monitor user systemd service without mutating the tracked service template, and it repairs previously rendered `NOTIFY_*` lines by key.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, running the real setup script with a temporary `HOME`; `systemctl` was stubbed so the script could be exercised safely without changing the host user services.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Ran `bash -n scripts/setup-auth-system.sh`.
2. Ran `git diff --check`.
3. Ran `scripts/setup-auth-system.sh` with a temporary `HOME`, an ntfy topic, and a redacted phone number.
4. Inspected the generated `~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-auth-monitor.service` under the temporary `HOME`.
5. Checked for leftover temporary service files.
6. Checked `git diff -- scripts/systemd/openclaw-auth-monitor.service` to verify the tracked template stayed unchanged.
7. Repeated the setup from a temp-copied old template that already had custom `Environment=NOTIFY_*=` values.
8. Ran the setup with a failing `python3` stub first on `PATH` to verify this path does not invoke `python3`.
9. Ran a template-drift failure check with an existing installed service file.

**Evidence after fix:**

```console
$ bash -n scripts/setup-auth-system.sh
$ git diff --check
$ HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw-auth-proof.../home PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-auth-proof.../bin:$PATH bash scripts/setup-auth-system.sh
...
Installing systemd timer...
Auth monitor installed and running.

--- installed service after normal run ---
[Unit]
Description=OpenClaw Auth Expiry Monitor
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart="/Users/ring/.openclaw/workspace/research/openclaw-pr-scout/openclaw/scripts/auth-monitor.sh"
; Configure notification channels via environment
Environment=WARN_HOURS=2
Environment=NOTIFY_PHONE=+15555550100
Environment=NOTIFY_NTFY=proof-topic

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

--- systemctl log ---
systemctl --user is-active openclaw
systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user enable --now openclaw-auth-monitor.timer

--- temp service files left ---

--- tracked template diff after normal run ---
```

Rerun proof against a temp-copied old template that already had custom notification values:

```console
--- installed service after old-custom-template rerun ---
[Unit]
Description=OpenClaw Auth Expiry Monitor
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart="/private/tmp/openclaw-auth-proof.../copy/scripts/auth-monitor.sh"
; Configure notification channels via environment
Environment=WARN_HOURS=2
Environment=NOTIFY_PHONE=+15555550200
Environment=NOTIFY_NTFY=new-topic

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target

--- old custom source template remains dirty only in temp copy ---
ExecStart=/home/admin/openclaw/scripts/auth-monitor.sh
; Configure notification channels via environment
Environment=WARN_HOURS=2
Environment=NOTIFY_PHONE=old-phone
Environment=NOTIFY_NTFY=old-topic
```

No-`python3` dependency proof: setup still succeeds with a failing `python3` stub first on `PATH`:

```console
Installing systemd timer...
Auth monitor installed and running.

--- installed service after normal run with python3 stub first on PATH ---
ExecStart="/Users/ring/.openclaw/workspace/research/openclaw-pr-scout/openclaw/scripts/auth-monitor.sh"
Environment=NOTIFY_PHONE=+15555550100
Environment=NOTIFY_NTFY=proof-topic
```

Template-drift proof: existing installed service remains untouched if a required placeholder is missing:

```console
Installing systemd timer...
ERROR: ExecStart placeholder not found in /private/tmp/openclaw-auth-proof.../openclaw-auth-monitor.service.XXXXXX

exit_status=1
--- existing service after template drift ---
EXISTING_WORKING_UNIT

--- temp service files left after template drift ---
```

**Observed result after fix:** The generated user service contains the rendered checkout-local `ExecStart` and configured notification values, no temporary service file remains, and the tracked `scripts/systemd/openclaw-auth-monitor.service` template has no diff. The old-custom-template rerun replaces existing `NOTIFY_*` lines by key. The setup path does not invoke `python3`. A required-placeholder mismatch exits before overwriting the existing installed service.

**What was not tested:** I did not run the timer end-to-end on a live Linux systemd user session in this environment.

## Scope boundary
This PR only fixes how the auth-monitor systemd unit is materialized during setup.

It does not:
- change auth-monitor runtime behavior
- change notification routing
- change gateway/systemd unit naming
- refactor other helper scripts

## Validation
- `bash -n scripts/setup-auth-system.sh`
- `git diff --check`
- ran the setup script with a temporary `HOME` and stubbed `systemctl`
- verified that the generated user unit contains the configured environment values
- verified that the generated user unit points to the current checkout's `scripts/auth-monitor.sh`
- verified that no temporary service file remains after install
- verified that the tracked `scripts/systemd/openclaw-auth-monitor.service` template remains unchanged
- verified a rerun against a temp-copied old template with pre-rendered custom `NOTIFY_*` values
- verified setup succeeds with a failing `python3` stub first on `PATH`
- verified that a required-placeholder mismatch exits before overwriting an existing service unit

## User impact
Users can run the setup script without dirtying the repo or generating a user unit with a machine-specific template path.

## Risks and mitigations
- Risk: the rendered `ExecStart` path could be wrong if the copied unit is patched incorrectly
- Mitigation: validate required placeholders before installing the rendered unit
- Risk: setup failure could replace a previously working user service
- Mitigation: render to a temporary unit and move it over the installed unit only after rendering succeeds

## Compatibility / migration
- backward compatible: yes
- migration required: no

## Failure recovery
- rerun `scripts/setup-auth-system.sh` to regenerate the user unit
- restore the previous unit file from `~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-auth-monitor.service` if needed

target.title: [perf] improve reply send performance
target.body:
## Summary

- Duplicate of #85853, rebased onto current `openclaw/openclaw:main` at `0ba6b235349add2d1d47b6543b980ee90d93a0df`.
- Keeps both commits from the original stack:
  - `fix: avoid blocking reply sends on bookkeeping`
  - `fix telegram progress tool result preview`
- Improves reply latency by avoiding dispatcher-idle waits on every direct block reply callback.
- Flushes pending block delivery only before subsequent tool/progress events, preserving block-before-tool ordering without making lightweight replies wait on dispatcher bookkeeping.
- Schedules source reply transcript mirroring after successful `message.action` sends without blocking the gateway response, while serializing async mirrors per session to preserve transcript chronology.
- Sends Telegram direct-message typing earlier after access checks so delayed context/session construction does not postpone the visible typing cue.

## Regression Source

#83722 is the merged regression source. It correctly fixed same-channel pre-tool block ordering by waiting for block reply delivery before tool execution could continue, but the merged implementation placed that delivery barrier directly on the hot block-reply callback path.

That means lightweight/direct replies can wait for dispatcher idle or delivery bookkeeping even when no later tool/progress event is about to be emitted. Under concurrent embedded runs, cron work, slow channel I/O, or transcript mirroring pressure, the ordering fix from #83722 turns into amplified user-visible reply latency.

This PR keeps the ordering guarantee introduced by #83722, but moves the wait to the point where ordering matters: before a later tool/progress emission is sent. Direct block replies can resolve without waiting on dispatcher idle, while a subsequent event still flushes pending block delivery first.

## Impact

Simple visible replies can return sooner without compromising user-visible send order. ACP direct block replies remain non-blocking for the common path, block-before-tool/progress chronology is preserved, transcript mirroring stays best-effort after successful source sends, and Telegram direct typing begins earlier without adding a duplicate immediate cue.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: direct visible replies should not be delayed by dispatcher idle waits, source transcript mirroring, or late Telegram typing setup while preserving block-before-tool/progress ordering.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on this rebased branch, with `origin/main` fetched to `0ba6b235349add2d1d47b6543b980ee90d93a0df` before PR creation.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: branch contains exactly the two duplicated commits on top of current `origin/main`; `git diff --check` was clean; focused Vitest passed 7 files / 381 tests.

Observed result after fix: reply dispatch, ACP delivery, gateway send transcript mirroring, Telegram typing, bot message, and Telegram dispatch regression coverage all passed on the rebased stack.

What was not tested: live Telegram/WhatsApp/Discord account roundtrip with real external credentials.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.typing.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`

target.title: Add per-agent sandbox session visibility override feature
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandboxed session tools always applied the default sandbox session visibility clamp before considering the requesting agent, so `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` could be configured but was not consistently honored.
- Runtime impact: operators could not safely give one named sandboxed agent full configured session visibility while keeping other sandboxed agents clamped to spawned sessions.
- Change: session visibility resolution now derives the requester agent first, checks that agent's sandbox override, and falls back to `agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` only when no per-agent override exists.
- Compatibility: existing configs keep the same default `"spawned"` clamp unless a named agent explicitly sets `sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility`.

## User-Visible Behavior

New optional per-agent config:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "spawned" }
    },
    "list": [
      { "id": "tony", "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "all" } }
    ]
  }
}
```

For sandboxed session tools, precedence is:

```text
agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility
agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility
default spawned clamp
```

## Security Impact

- Data access scope can broaden only for an explicitly named sandboxed agent.
- The global/default sandbox behavior remains clamped to spawned sessions.
- Existing `tools.sessions.visibility` and `tools.agentToAgent` policy checks still apply; this does not bypass cross-agent policy.
- No secrets, credentials, network calls, shell execution, or gateway auth behavior changed.

## Files in This PR

- `CHANGELOG.md` - records the user-facing runtime/config behavior change.
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` - documents per-agent sandbox session visibility.
- `docs/gateway/config-tools.md` - documents session tool visibility and sandbox override precedence.
- `docs/tools/multi-agent-sandbox-tools.md` - documents multi-agent sandbox session tool behavior.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts` - wires requester identity into session tool construction.
- `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware sandbox visibility to `session_status`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts` - centralizes requester-aware sandbox visibility resolution.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` - forwards requester context through shared session helper wiring.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_history`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_list`.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` - applies requester-aware visibility to `sessions_send`.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` - covers requester-aware `session_status` behavior.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts` - covers tool-factory wiring for the override.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts` - covers end-to-end session tool behavior through the OpenClaw tool factory.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts` - covers access-layer override/fallback resolution.

## Verification

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=360000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts --reporter=dot`
  - Result: 10 test files passed, 268 tests passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: a sandboxed named agent with `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility="all"` can use the configured `tools.sessions.visibility` scope, while sandboxed agents without that override remain clamped to spawned sessions.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout using production `createOpenClawTools`, embedded OpenClaw gateway session handlers, and real temporary `sessions.json` stores for `main` and `tony`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module -e <inline proof script>` from the repo root. The script created an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, wrote real per-agent session stores, enabled embedded mode, constructed sandboxed `sessions_list` tools for `main` and `tony`, and compared observed session keys with expected visibility.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "config": {
    "toolsSessionsVisibility": "all",
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] },
    "defaultSandboxSessionToolsVisibility": "spawned",
    "tonySandboxSessionToolsVisibility": "all"
  },
  "observed": {
    "mainSandboxedKeys": ["agent:main:subagent:main-child"],
    "tonySandboxedKeys": [
      "agent:main:main",
      "agent:main:subagent:main-child",
      "agent:tony:main",
      "agent:tony:subagent:tony-child"
    ]
  },
  "result": "PASS"
}
```

Observed result after fix: `main` stayed clamped to spawned sessions through the default sandbox setting; `tony` honored the per-agent `"all"` override and saw the full configured session scope when `tools.agentToAgent` allowed cross-agent access.

What was not tested: remote Crabbox/Testbox behavior. Crabbox is not installed in this shell, and the available Blacksmith CLI is unauthenticated.

## Risk and Rollback

- Risk: an operator may grant broad session visibility to the wrong named agent.
- Mitigation: the override is explicit per agent, default behavior is unchanged, and cross-agent policy remains enforced.
- Rollback: remove `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` from the agent entry to return to the default sandbox clamp.

target.title: fix(feishu): guard against undefined receiveIdType in resolveFeishuSendTarget
target.body:
## What

resolveReceiveIdType() previously fell back to "user_id" for any unrecognized ID format, silently sending the wrong ID type to the Feishu API and causing 400 "receive_id_type is required" errors on newer Lark SDK versions (which silently drop undefined fields).

Now returns null for unrecognized formats, forcing all callers to handle the error explicitly with a descriptive throw rather than propagating invalid state.

## Root Cause

Bot review correctly identified that resolveReceiveIdType never returned falsy — it unconditionally returned "user_id" as a fallback. Unknown ID formats were silently treated as user_id, which the Feishu API would then reject.

## How

- targets.ts: resolveReceiveIdType returns null | "chat_id" | "open_id" | "user_id" — returns null instead of "user_id" for unrecognized formats
- send-target.ts: guard + throw when receiveIdType is null
- reply-dispatcher.ts: guard + early return when receiveIdType is null

Fixes #53376

target.title: fix(media): sanitize fetch errors before posting to channels
target.body:
## Summary

When a media fetch operation fails, the raw error message ├ö├ç├Â which may contain serialized JSON with private group IDs, phone numbers, user names, and internal system paths ├ö├ç├Â was posted to the configured channel as if it were a normal reply. This exposed PII to public-facing channels like Discord and Telegram.

**Example of what was leaking:**
```
Media failed: {"group_id":"@g.us","sender":"+905...","name":"John Doe","path":"/home/user/.openclaw/..."}
```

## Details

Added `SAFE_MEDIA_FETCH_ERROR_MESSAGE` constant and `isSafeMediaFetchError()` helper to `src/media/fetch.ts`. When a `MediaFetchError` is detected at the outbound delivery layer (`message-action-params.ts`, `message-action-runner.ts`, `deliver.ts`), the raw error is replaced with:

```
├ö├£├í┬┤┬®├à Media fetch failed. The attachment could not be retrieved.
```

The original error is still logged internally via `logVerbose` ├ö├ç├Â it is only redacted from the outbound channel payload.

Three sanitization boundary points:
1. `message-action-params.ts` ├ö├ç├Â params resolution for media actions
2. `message-action-runner.ts` ├ö├ç├Â action execution runner
3. `deliver.ts` ├ö├ç├Â final delivery layer

## Related Issues

Fixes #20279

## How to Validate

Trigger a media fetch that fails (invalid URL or timeout). Confirm:
- Channel receives the generic safe message
- `logVerbose` / internal logs still contain the full error

Run unit tests:
```bash
pnpm test -- --testPathPattern="fetch|message-action-runner.media"
```

Note: `message-action-runner.media.test.ts` requires `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` which was added to `main` today (commit `6e20c4baa0`). The test will pass once dependencies are installed in CI. The `fetch.test.ts` suite (12 tests) passes locally and covers the core PII sanitization behavior.

## Pre-Merge Checklist

- [x] Updated relevant documentation and README (if needed)
- [x] Added/updated tests (if needed)
- [ ] Noted breaking changes (if any)
- [x] Validated on required platforms/methods:

target.title: fix(models): log warning and continue when one provider fails validation
target.body:
## Summary

When `models.providers` contains a validation error in any single provider (e.g., a custom `models` array with a missing `apiKey`), the entire `models.json` config was silently rejected. This caused all custom `baseUrl` overrides and model definitions to be lost without any warning ├ö├ç├Â often unnoticed until requests started failing against the wrong endpoints.

## Details

Added per-provider validation isolation in `src/config/validation.ts`. When a provider-level validation error is detected:
- A clear warning is logged identifying the provider key and the specific validation error
- The invalid provider is skipped
- All other valid providers continue to load normally

**Before:**
```
[silent] All custom models and baseUrl overrides lost
```

**After:**
```
├ö├£├í┬┤┬®├à Provider "minimax" failed validation: "apiKey" is required when defining custom models. Skipping this provider.
```

## Related Issues

Fixes #21584

## How to Validate

1. Configure two providers: one valid (with custom `baseUrl`), one invalid (missing `apiKey`)
2. Start the gateway
3. Confirm the valid provider uses its configured `baseUrl`
4. Check logs ├ö├ç├Â warning shows which provider was skipped and why

Run unit tests: `pnpm test -- --testPathPattern=validation.provider-sanitize`

target.title: fix(paths): expand env placeholders in resolveUserPath
target.body:
## Summary
Fix path resolution so environment placeholders are expanded before filesystem resolution.

This addresses issue #53628 where `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}` in configured path strings was treated as a literal directory name.

## Changes
- Add environment placeholder expansion in `src/infra/home-dir.ts`.
  - Support `${VAR_NAME}` and `$VAR_NAME` forms.
  - Keep unknown placeholders unchanged.
  - Preserve existing `~` home expansion behavior.
- Add tests in `src/infra/home-dir.test.ts`:
  - braced env placeholder expansion
  - bare env placeholder expansion
  - unknown placeholder passthrough

## Why this fixes the bug
`resolveUserPath` delegates to `resolveHomeRelativePath`. After this change, inputs like:
- `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}/workspace/skills`
- `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/workspace/skills`
are resolved using `process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (or provided env), so skill installation no longer writes into a literal `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}` folder.

## Testing
```bash
npx vitest run src/infra/home-dir.test.ts src/utils.test.ts
```
Result: pass (`3 files, 51 tests`).

## Risk / boundaries
- Scope is limited to path normalization helpers.
- Unknown env placeholders are intentionally left unchanged.
- Existing behavior for plain paths and tilde expansion is preserved.

target.title: feat(gateway): add watchdog + startup error diagnostics (closes #53684)
target.body:
## Summary

Implements **#53684 - Gateway failure recovery and notification mechanism**.

The PR adds three complementary layers to ensure the OpenClaw Gateway can recover from crashes and notify operators:

### 1. Startup error diagnostics ()

When an uncaught exception or fatal unhandled rejection occurs before or during gateway startup, the error is written to  *before* the process exits. This makes post-mortem diagnosis reliable — the error file survives even when the terminal buffer is gone.

### 2. External watchdog ()

A lightweight bash watchdog designed to be run via  (macOS) or  (Linux). It polls the gateway's  endpoint and:
- Attempts a restart (up to 3 tries) if the gateway is down
- Sends a **macOS Notification** () or **Linux Notification** () when things go wrong
- Logs all events to 

Run it with:
```bash
openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 3
```

### 3. CLI integration

`openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 5` spawns the watchdog script as a foreground child process, forwarding stdout/stderr and handling SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanly.

---

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/infra/startup-error.ts` | New — writes startup errors to `logs/startup-error.txt` |
| `src/infra/unhandled-rejections.ts` | Wire `writeStartupError` into fatal unhandled rejections |
| `src/cli/run-main.ts` | Write startup errors for pre-gateway uncaught exceptions |
| `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts` | Add `gateway watchdog` subcommand |
| `src/cli/gateway-cli/register.ts` | Register watchdog command |
| `scripts/openclaw-watchdog.sh` | New — bash watchdog script |
| `src/infra/startup-error.ts` | Fix lint: unused `format` import removed |

## Testing

```bash
# Local build
pnpm install && pnpm run build

# Run the watchdog (foreground test)
openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 1 --max-attempts 1 --no-notify

# Check startup error file (after forcing a crash):
# ~/.openclaw/logs/startup-error.txt
```

---

cc @openclaw/maintainers

target.title: fix: preserve approved heredoc commands on gateway exec
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve approved heredoc commands as-authored in the gateway exec approval flow
- avoid routing approved heredoc commands through the rebuilt allowlist wrapper command
- fixes approval-gated multiline commands like `osascript <<'APPLESCRIPT' ... APPLESCRIPT` failing with `No such file or directory`

## Root cause
The gateway exec approval path rebuilt allowlist-satisfied commands into `enforcedCommand`. That rebuild does not preserve heredoc bodies correctly, so once a heredoc command was approved it could execute in a mangled form.

## Fix
When the command required heredoc-specific approval, execute the original command text (`params.command`) instead of the rebuilt wrapper command. Keep the rebuilt command path for non-heredoc cases.

## Validation
- reproduced locally with an approval-gated AppleScript heredoc
- smoke-tested plain shell heredocs and `osascript` heredocs after the fix

target.title: fix: group and thread sessions default to idle reset instead of daily
target.body:
## Problem

OpenClaw resets all session types on a **daily** schedule by default. For DMs this makes sense — a fresh start each morning is reasonable.

But for group channels and forum topics/threads, daily resets are actively harmful:
- A Telegram forum topic in active use gets its context wiped at 4am, even mid-conversation
- Discord threads spanning multiple days lose all history at midnight
- Slack threads reset between working sessions on consecutive days

The result: the agent wakes up every morning with no memory of the thread it's been participating in, forcing users to re-explain context constantly.

This was raised in #32109.

## Root Cause

`resolveSessionResetPolicy()` in `reset.ts` uses `DEFAULT_RESET_MODE = "daily"` unconditionally when no explicit policy is configured. The `resetType` parameter (`"direct"` / `"group"` / `"thread"`) is already correctly derived and passed in — but it wasn't being used to vary the default.

## Fix

When no explicit `session.resetByType` config is set:
- **Group and thread sessions** → default to `idle` mode with a **7-day window** (10080 minutes)
- **Direct/DM sessions** → unchanged, still default to `daily`

7 days matches Discord's own maximum thread archive duration and is long enough to survive a weekend without losing context, while still expiring genuinely stale threads.

Explicit `session.resetByType.group` / `session.resetByType.thread` config always takes precedence — this only changes the implicit default.

## Changes

- `reset.ts`: add `DEFAULT_GROUP_THREAD_IDLE_MINUTES = 10080`, use `impliedDefaultMode` based on session type
- `sessions.test.ts`: update the group policy test to reflect the new correct default (`idle`, not `daily`), and add assertion that `dm`-specific `idleMinutes` does not bleed into group sessions

## Fixes

Fixes #32109

target.title: fix(session): add postRotationStartupUntilMs for Discord channel and thread resets
target.body:
## Summary

Reworks the Discord post-reset startup grace window on top of current `main` and consolidates the overlap with #49001.

This keeps the existing goal of #53524: after an explicit `/new` or `/reset` in a Discord channel or thread, the first owner message that gets queued behind an active startup/run can be wrapped with startup-recovery instructions instead of being treated as an ordinary background message.

## Changes

- Add `postRotationStartupUntilMs` to `SessionEntry`.
- Set a 30s post-rotation startup window only for explicit Discord channel/thread resets.
- When an owner message is steered into an active embedded run during that window:
  - wrap it with startup-recovery instructions;
  - preserve the currently active reply-run session id when queueing;
  - clear the window before returning from the successful embedded steer enqueue.
- If the first owner message proceeds as a normal run instead of a queued steer, clear the window only after that owner run actually starts.
- Do not let non-owner messages consume the window.

## Review blockers addressed

- Clears the window only when an owner turn consumes it; non-owner queued messages leave it intact.
- Clears the window before returning from successful embedded steer enqueue.
- Preserves the active session id by using `replyRunRegistry.resolveSessionId(sessionKey)` before falling back to the follow-up run session id.
- Rebased/reworked on current `main`; PR is no longer conflicting.

## Tests

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.misc.runreplyagent.test.ts

target.title: feat(slack): add ignoreOtherMentions channel config
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `ignoreOtherMentions` option for Slack channel config, mirroring the existing Discord guild/channel feature
- When enabled, messages that mention another user (`<@U...>`) but do not explicitly mention the bot are dropped — even inside bot-participated threads
- This prevents the bot from replying when a user is talking to a coworker in a thread where the bot previously responded

## Slack vs Discord difference

Discord's `ignoreOtherMentions` checks `!wasMentioned && !implicitMention`. The Slack version intentionally omits the `!implicitMention` check because Slack's implicit mention (via `hasSlackThreadParticipation`) fires for **every** message in any bot-participated thread, which would make the feature ineffective.

## Config example

```json5
{
  channels: {
    slack: {
      channels: {
        "*": { ignoreOtherMentions: true },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Changed files

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/types.slack.ts` | Add `ignoreOtherMentions` to `SlackChannelConfig` |
| `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` | Add zod validation for the new field |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/channel-config.ts` | Wire through type + resolver |
| `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts` | Drop logic before mention gating |

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build` passes
- [x] `pnpm test -- extensions/slack/src/monitor` — 236 tests pass
- [x] Manual test on live Slack workspace: bot no longer replies when tagging a coworker in a bot thread
- [x] Bot still replies when explicitly @mentioned in the same thread

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(codex): ensure codex subagent bootstrap parity with pi subagents - only inject AGENTS.md and TOOLS.md
target.body:
## Summary

This PR makes Codex-native subagents follow the same workspace-bootstrap boundary introduced for OpenClaw subagents in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/56308a71447972b3913e9583d190f9038722d4b2.

That earlier change narrowed ordinary OpenClaw subagent context from:

- `AGENTS.md`
- `SOUL.md`
- `IDENTITY.md`
- `USER.md`
- `TOOLS.md`

to only:
- `AGENTS.md`
- `TOOLS.md`

The goal is correct: subagents should receive the operational/project instructions they need, but not inherit parent-session persona, identity, user-profile, memory, heartbeat, or other full persistent-session context.

However, Codex-native subagents are spawned inside the Codex extension. They do not go through the normal OpenClaw subagent bootstrap path, so the https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/56308a71447972b3913e9583d190f9038722d4b2 boundary did not apply to them. In practice, Codex-native children inherit thread-level Codex developer instructions from the parent Codex thread. If parent-only workspace files are placed there, native Codex subagents clone them too.

This PR routes Codex workspace instructions so the Codex path matches the intended subagent boundary.

## Why this is needed

OpenClaw has two different subagent execution paths:

- OpenClaw-managed subagents, which use OpenClaw's own session/bootstrap assembly.
- Codex-native subagents, which are spawned by the Codex app-server extension.

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/56308a71447972b3913e9583d190f9038722d4b2 fixed the first path by narrowing subagent bootstrap files to `AGENTS.md` and `TOOLS.md`.

The Codex-native path needs a separate fix because Codex subagents inherit the parent Codex thread's developer instructions. Before this change, files such as `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md` could be placed in inherited thread developer instructions, which means Codex-native children could receive parent-session context that should remain parent-only.

That is both a context-boundary issue and a prompt-shaping issue:

- Subagents need tool/process guidance from `TOOLS.md`.
- Subagents should still get project guidance from Codex's native project-doc handling for `AGENTS.md`.
- Subagents should not inherit parent persona/user files such as `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, or `USER.md`.
- Parent turns still need those files, because they define the primary interactive agent's behavior and relationship to the user.

## How this solves it
This PR splits Codex workspace instruction delivery into two layers:

1. **Inherited Codex thread developer instructions**

   These are still inherited by Codex-native subagents.

   This layer now only carries `TOOLS.md`.

   `AGENTS.md` remains handled by Codex's native project-doc loading, so OpenClaw does not duplicate it into developer instructions.

2. **Turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions**

   These are attached to each parent turn through `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`.
   This layer carries the parent-only workspace files:

   - `SOUL.md`
   - `IDENTITY.md`
   - `USER.md`

   Because these instructions are turn-scoped, they apply to the current parent turn without becoming part of the inherited Codex thread developer instructions that native subagents clone.

This preserves the intended behavior:

- Parent Codex turns still receive the full parent context needed for the interactive agent.
- Codex-native subagents inherit `TOOLS.md`, but not `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, or `USER.md`.

## Additional details

The PR also preserves Codex Default-mode behavior when OpenClaw adds turn-scoped developer instructions.

Codex only applies its built-in Default-mode preset when turn-scoped `developer_instructions` is `null`. Once OpenClaw adds turn-scoped workspace instructions, that field is no longer null. To avoid accidentally dropping the Default-mode collaboration guidance, this PR explicitly prepends the Default-mode instructions before the OpenClaw turn-scoped workspace overlay.

The PR also updates prompt accounting/reporting so the rendered developer-instruction size includes both:

- inherited thread developer instructions, and
- turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions.

That keeps provider-boundary prechecks, system prompt reports, trajectory records, and LLM input hooks aligned with what Codex actually receives.

## Implementation notes

Main changes:

- Split workspace developer instruction selection into inherited and turn-scoped sets.
- Keep `TOOLS.md` in inherited Codex thread developer instructions.
- Move `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md` into turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions.
- Preserve Codex Default-mode collaboration instructions when turn-scoped OpenClaw instructions are present.
- Add separate renderers for:
  - thread workspace instructions, using `## OpenClaw Workspace Instructions`
  - parent collaboration persona/user instructions, using `## OpenClaw Agent Soul`
- Update system prompt reporting and token/precheck accounting to include both developer-instruction layers.
- Document that Codex-native subagents follow the same `AGENTS.md` + `TOOLS.md` boundary.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: native Codex subagents inherit only stable shared workspace guidance (`AGENTS.md`/`TOOLS.md`) through thread developer instructions; persona, identity, and user workspace files stay turn-scoped so cloned Codex subagent config does not receive them by default.
- Real environment tested: Crabbox AWS run `run_41291f907e34`, lease `cbx_0a6cc82b3c29`, slug `swift-shrimp`, `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini`, auth-profile mode, fake large `SOUL.md`/`IDENTITY.md`/`USER.md` files plus fake `AGENTS.md`/`TOOLS.md`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran two live Codex turns plus `/status` probes in the Crabbox setup; ran local `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`; ran local `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; ran local `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "passes stable workspace files|uses turn-scoped collaboration instructions"`; ran `git diff --check`; ran `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --engine codex --thinking low --no-web-search`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live output from Crabbox terminal:

  ```text
  provider=aws lease=cbx_0a6cc82b3c29 slug=swift-shrimp run=run_41291f907e34
  model=openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini auth_profile=openai-api-key
  fake_workspace_sizes: AGENTS=77 TOOLS=48 SOUL=57200 IDENTITY=59800 USER=57200
  turn1=OC_PROBE_ONE completed

  turn2=OC_PROBE_TWO completed
  store_after_turn2: codex_rollout_jsonl=272840 bytes trajectory=160485 bytes
  marker_counts_after_two_turns_and_two_status_calls:
    SOUL_BIG_CONTEXT_MARKER=675
    IDENTITY_BIG_CONTEXT_MARKER=645
    USER_BIG_CONTEXT_MARKER=675
    OPENCLAW_TOOL_MARKER=9
  result=large persona/user files did not replay as inherited thread config; TOOLS stayed shared
  ```

  Supplemental local proof: prompt snapshots current; 46 focused tests passed plus 3 targeted run-attempt tests passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
- Observed result after fix: both live turns completed, `TOOLS.md` stayed inherited/thread-level, persona/user files stayed turn-scoped, and native Codex subagent inheritance matches the intended bootstrap boundary.
- What was not tested: full repository check suite locally.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing pre-fix PR proof reported a parent Codex turn and fork_context:false gpt-5.4-mini subagent both seeing actual `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, and `TOOLS.md` content in inherited developer context.
